results.description: The Assets class initializes the partition during construction. It sets up the necessary storage partition for managing assets like audio files, images, or other resources used by the application. This ensures that all required assets are properly loaded and accessible when needed.

21:     uint32_t asset_size;   /*!< Size of the asset */
22:     uint32_t asset_offset; /*!< Offset of the asset */

39: uint32_t Assets::CalculateChecksum(const char *data, uint32_t length)
40: {
41:     uint32_t checksum = 0;
42:     for (uint32_t i = 0; i < length; i++)
43:     {
44:         checksum += data[i];
45:     }
46:     return checksum & 0xFFFF;
47: }

49: bool Assets::InitializePartition()
50: {
51:     partition_valid_ = false;
52:     checksum_valid_ = false;
53:     assets_.clear();
54: 
55:     partition_ = esp_partition_find_first(ESP_PARTITION_TYPE_ANY, ESP_PARTITION_SUBTYPE_ANY, "assets");
56:     if (partition_ == nullptr)
57:     {
58:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No assets partition found");
59:         return false;
60:     }
61: 
62:     int free_pages = spi_flash_mmap_get_free_pages(SPI_FLASH_MMAP_DATA);
63:     uint32_t storage_size = free_pages * 64 * 1024;
64:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "The storage free size is %ld KB", storage_size / 1024);
65:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "The partition size is %ld KB", partition_->size / 1024);
66:     if (storage_size < partition_->size)
67:     {
68:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "The free size %ld KB is less than assets partition required %ld KB", storage_size / 1024, partition_->size / 1024);
69:         return false;
70:     }
71: 
72:     esp_err_t err = esp_partition_mmap(partition_, 0, partition_->size, ESP_PARTITION_MMAP_DATA, (const void **)&mmap_root_, &mmap_handle_);
73:     if (err != ESP_OK)
74:     {
75:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to mmap assets partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
76:         return false;
77:     }
78: 
79:     partition_valid_ = true;
80: 
81:     uint32_t stored_files = *(uint32_t *)(mmap_root_ + 0);
82:     uint32_t stored_chksum = *(uint32_t *)(mmap_root_ + 4);
83:     uint32_t stored_len = *(uint32_t *)(mmap_root_ + 8);
84: 
85:     if (stored_len > partition_->size - 12)
86:     {
87:         ESP_LOGD(TAG, "The stored_len (0x%lx) is greater than the partition size (0x%lx) - 12", stored_len, partition_->size);
88:         return false;
89:     }
90: 
91:     auto start_time = esp_timer_get_time();
92:     uint32_t calculated_checksum = CalculateChecksum(mmap_root_ + 12, stored_len);
93:     auto end_time = esp_timer_get_time();
94:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "The checksum calculation time is %d ms", int((end_time - start_time) / 1000));
95: 
96:     if (calculated_checksum != stored_chksum)
97:     {
98:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "The calculated checksum (0x%lx) does not match the stored checksum (0x%lx)", calculated_checksum, stored_chksum);
99:         return false;
100:     }
101: 
102:     checksum_valid_ = true;
103: 
104:     for (uint32_t i = 0; i < stored_files; i++)
105:     {
106:         auto item = (const mmap_assets_table *)(mmap_root_ + 12 + i * sizeof(mmap_assets_table));
107:         auto asset = Asset{
108:             .size = static_cast<size_t>(item->asset_size),
109:             .offset = static_cast<size_t>(12 + sizeof(mmap_assets_table) * stored_files + item->asset_offset)};
110:         assets_[item->asset_name] = asset;
111:     }
112:     return checksum_valid_;
113: }

463: bool Assets::Download(std::string url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> progress_callback)
464: {
465:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Downloading new version of assets from %s", url.c_str());
466: 
467:     // 取消当前资源分区的内存映射
468:     if (mmap_handle_ != 0)
469:     {
470:         esp_partition_munmap(mmap_handle_);
471:         mmap_handle_ = 0;
472:         mmap_root_ = nullptr;
473:     }
474:     checksum_valid_ = false;
475:     assets_.clear();
476: 
477:     // 下载新的资源文件
478:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
479:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
480: 
481:     if (!http->Open("GET", url))
482:     {
483:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
484:         return false;
485:     }
486: 
487:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
488:     {
489:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get assets, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
490:         return false;
491:     }
492: 
493:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
494:     if (content_length == 0)
495:     {
496:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
497:         return false;
498:     }
499: 
500:     if (content_length > partition_->size)
501:     {
502:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Assets file size (%u) is larger than partition size (%lu)", content_length, partition_->size);
503:         return false;
504:     }
505: 
506:     // 定义扇区大小为4KB（ESP32的标准扇区大小）
507:     const size_t SECTOR_SIZE = esp_partition_get_main_flash_sector_size();
508: 
509:     // 计算需要擦除的扇区数量
510:     size_t sectors_to_erase = (content_length + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE; // 向上取整
511:     size_t total_erase_size = sectors_to_erase * SECTOR_SIZE;
512: 
513:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Sector size: %u, content length: %u, sectors to erase: %u, total erase size: %u",
514:              SECTOR_SIZE, content_length, sectors_to_erase, total_erase_size);
515: 
516:     // 写入新的资源文件到分区，一边erase一边写入
517:     char buffer[512];
518:     size_t total_written = 0;
519:     size_t recent_written = 0;
520:     size_t current_sector = 0;
521:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
522: 
523:     while (true)
524:     {
525:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
526:         if (ret < 0)
527:         {
528:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
529:             return false;
530:         }
531: 
532:         if (ret == 0)
533:         {
534:             break;
535:         }
536: 
537:         // 检查是否需要擦除新的扇区
538:         size_t write_end_offset = total_written + ret;
539:         size_t needed_sectors = (write_end_offset + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE;
540: 
541:         // 擦除需要的新扇区
542:         while (current_sector < needed_sectors)
543:         {
544:             size_t sector_start = current_sector * SECTOR_SIZE;
545:             size_t sector_end = (current_sector + 1) * SECTOR_SIZE;
546: 
547:             // 确保擦除范围不超过分区大小
548:             if (sector_end > partition_->size)
549:             {
550:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Sector end (%u) exceeds partition size (%lu)", sector_end, partition_->size);
551:                 return false;
552:             }
553: 
554:             ESP_LOGD(TAG, "Erasing sector %u (offset: %u, size: %u)", current_sector, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
555:             esp_err_t err = esp_partition_erase_range(partition_, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
556:             if (err != ESP_OK)
557:             {
558:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to erase sector %u at offset %u: %s", current_sector, sector_start, esp_err_to_name(err));
559:                 return false;
560:             }
561: 
562:             current_sector++;
563:         }
564: 
565:         // 写入数据到分区
566:         esp_err_t err = esp_partition_write(partition_, total_written, buffer, ret);
567:         if (err != ESP_OK)
568:         {
569:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write to assets partition at offset %u: %s", total_written, esp_err_to_name(err));
570:             return false;
571:         }
572: 
573:         total_written += ret;
574:         recent_written += ret;
575: 
576:         // 计算进度和速度
577:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || total_written == content_length || ret == 0)
578:         {
579:             size_t progress = total_written * 100 / content_length;
580:             size_t speed = recent_written; // 每秒的字节数
581:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %u B/s, Sectors erased: %u",
582:                      progress, total_written, content_length, speed, current_sector);
583:             if (progress_callback)
584:             {
585:                 progress_callback(progress, speed);
586:             }
587:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
588:             recent_written = 0; // 重置最近写入的字节数
589:         }
590:     }
591: 
592:     http->Close();
593: 
594:     if (total_written != content_length)
595:     {
596:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Downloaded size (%u) does not match expected size (%u)", total_written, content_length);
597:         return false;
598:     }
599: 
600:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Assets download completed, total written: %u bytes, total sectors erased: %u",
601:              total_written, current_sector);
602: 
603:     // 重新初始化资源分区
604:     if (!InitializePartition())
605:     {
606:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to re-initialize assets partition");
607:         return false;
608:     }
609: 
610:     return true;
611: }

613: bool Assets::GetAssetData(const std::string &name, void *&ptr, size_t &size)
614: {
615:     auto asset = assets_.find(name);
616:     if (asset == assets_.end())
617:     {
618:         return false;
619:     }
620:     auto data = (const char *)(mmap_root_ + asset->second.offset);
621:     if (data[0] != 'Z' || data[1] != 'Z')
622:     {
623:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "The asset %s is not valid with magic %02x%02x", name.c_str(), data[0], data[1]);
624:         return false;
625:     }
626: 
627:     ptr = static_cast<void *>(const_cast<char *>(data + 2));
628:     size = asset->second.size;
629:     return true;
630: }

461: bool Assets::Download(std::string url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> progress_callback)
462: {
463:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Downloading new version of assets from %s", url.c_str());
464: 
465:     // 取消当前资源分区的内存映射
466:     if (mmap_handle_ != 0)
467:     {
468:         esp_partition_munmap(mmap_handle_);
469:         mmap_handle_ = 0;
470:         mmap_root_ = nullptr;
471:     }
472:     checksum_valid_ = false;
473:     assets_.clear();
474: 
475:     // 下载新的资源文件
476:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
477:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
478: 
479:     if (!http->Open("GET", url))
480:     {
481:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
482:         return false;
483:     }
484: 
485:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
486:     {
487:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get assets, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
488:         return false;
489:     }
490: 
491:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
492:     if (content_length == 0)
493:     {
494:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
495:         return false;
496:     }
497: 
498:     if (content_length > partition_->size)
499:     {
500:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Assets file size (%u) is larger than partition size (%lu)", content_length, partition_->size);
501:         return false;
502:     }
503: 
504:     // 定义扇区大小为4KB（ESP32的标准扇区大小）
505:     const size_t SECTOR_SIZE = esp_partition_get_main_flash_sector_size();
506: 
507:     // 计算需要擦除的扇区数量
508:     size_t sectors_to_erase = (content_length + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE; // 向上取整
509:     size_t total_erase_size = sectors_to_erase * SECTOR_SIZE;
510: 
511:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Sector size: %u, content length: %u, sectors to erase: %u, total erase size: %u",
512:              SECTOR_SIZE, content_length, sectors_to_erase, total_erase_size);
513: 
514:     // 写入新的资源文件到分区，一边erase一边写入
515:     char buffer[512];
516:     size_t total_written = 0;
517:     size_t recent_written = 0;
518:     size_t current_sector = 0;
519:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
520: 
521:     while (true)
522:     {
523:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
524:         if (ret < 0)
525:         {
526:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
527:             return false;
528:         }
529: 
530:         if (ret == 0)
531:         {
532:             break;
533:         }
534: 
535:         // 检查是否需要擦除新的扇区
536:         size_t write_end_offset = total_written + ret;
537:         size_t needed_sectors = (write_end_offset + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE;
538: 
539:         // 擦除需要的新扇区
540:         while (current_sector < needed_sectors)
541:         {
542:             size_t sector_start = current_sector * SECTOR_SIZE;
543:             size_t sector_end = (current_sector + 1) * SECTOR_SIZE;
544: 
545:             // 确保擦除范围不超过分区大小
546:             if (sector_end > partition_->size)
547:             {
548:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Sector end (%u) exceeds partition size (%lu)", sector_end, partition_->size);
549:                 return false;
550:             }
551: 
552:             ESP_LOGD(TAG, "Erasing sector %u (offset: %u, size: %u)", current_sector, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
553:             esp_err_t err = esp_partition_erase_range(partition_, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
554:             if (err != ESP_OK)
555:             {
556:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to erase sector %u at offset %u: %s", current_sector, sector_start, esp_err_to_name(err));
557:                 return false;
558:             }
559: 
560:             current_sector++;
561:         }
562: 
563:         // 写入数据到分区
564:         esp_err_t err = esp_partition_write(partition_, total_written, buffer, ret);
565:         if (err != ESP_OK)
566:         {
567:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write to assets partition at offset %u: %s", total_written, esp_err_to_name(err));
568:             return false;
569:         }
570: 
571:         total_written += ret;
572:         recent_written += ret;
573: 
574:         // 计算进度和速度
575:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || total_written == content_length || ret == 0)
576:         {
577:             size_t progress = total_written * 100 / content_length;
578:             size_t speed = recent_written; // 每秒的字节数
579:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %u B/s, Sectors erased: %u",
580:                      progress, total_written, content_length, speed, current_sector);
581:             if (progress_callback)
582:             {
583:                 progress_callback(progress, speed);
584:             }
585:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
586:             recent_written = 0; // 重置最近写入的字节数
587:         }
588:     }
589: 
590:     http->Close();
591: 
592:     if (total_written != content_length)
593:     {
594:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Downloaded size (%u) does not match expected size (%u)", total_written, content_length);
595:         return false;
596:     }
597: 
598:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Assets download completed, total written: %u bytes, total sectors erased: %u",
599:              total_written, current_sector);
600: 
601:     // 重新初始化资源分区
602:     if (!InitializePartition())
603:     {
604:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to re-initialize assets partition");
605:         return false;
606:     }
607: 
608:     return true;
609: }

611: bool Assets::GetAssetData(const std::string &name, void *&ptr, size_t &size)
612: {
613:     auto asset = assets_.find(name);
614:     if (asset == assets_.end())
615:     {
616:         return false;
617:     }
618:     auto data = (const char *)(mmap_root_ + asset->second.offset);
619:     if (data[0] != 'Z' || data[1] != 'Z')
620:     {
621:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "The asset %s is not valid with magic %02x%02x", name.c_str(), data[0], data[1]);
622:         return false;
623:     }
624: 
625:     ptr = static_cast<void *>(const_cast<char *>(data + 2));
626:     size = asset->second.size;
627:     return true;
628: }

459: bool Assets::Download(std::string url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> progress_callback)
460: {
461:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Downloading new version of assets from %s", url.c_str());
462: 
463:     // 取消当前资源分区的内存映射
464:     if (mmap_handle_ != 0)
465:     {
466:         esp_partition_munmap(mmap_handle_);
467:         mmap_handle_ = 0;
468:         mmap_root_ = nullptr;
469:     }
470:     checksum_valid_ = false;
471:     assets_.clear();
472: 
473:     // 下载新的资源文件
474:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
475:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
476: 
477:     if (!http->Open("GET", url))
478:     {
479:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
480:         return false;
481:     }
482: 
483:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
484:     {
485:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get assets, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
486:         return false;
487:     }
488: 
489:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
490:     if (content_length == 0)
491:     {
492:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
493:         return false;
494:     }
495: 
496:     if (content_length > partition_->size)
497:     {
498:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Assets file size (%u) is larger than partition size (%lu)", content_length, partition_->size);
499:         return false;
500:     }
501: 
502:     // 定义扇区大小为4KB（ESP32的标准扇区大小）
503:     const size_t SECTOR_SIZE = esp_partition_get_main_flash_sector_size();
504: 
505:     // 计算需要擦除的扇区数量
506:     size_t sectors_to_erase = (content_length + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE; // 向上取整
507:     size_t total_erase_size = sectors_to_erase * SECTOR_SIZE;
508: 
509:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Sector size: %u, content length: %u, sectors to erase: %u, total erase size: %u",
510:              SECTOR_SIZE, content_length, sectors_to_erase, total_erase_size);
511: 
512:     // 写入新的资源文件到分区，一边erase一边写入
513:     char buffer[512];
514:     size_t total_written = 0;
515:     size_t recent_written = 0;
516:     size_t current_sector = 0;
517:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
518: 
519:     while (true)
520:     {
521:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
522:         if (ret < 0)
523:         {
524:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
525:             return false;
526:         }
527: 
528:         if (ret == 0)
529:         {
530:             break;
531:         }
532: 
533:         // 检查是否需要擦除新的扇区
534:         size_t write_end_offset = total_written + ret;
535:         size_t needed_sectors = (write_end_offset + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE;
536: 
537:         // 擦除需要的新扇区
538:         while (current_sector < needed_sectors)
539:         {
540:             size_t sector_start = current_sector * SECTOR_SIZE;
541:             size_t sector_end = (current_sector + 1) * SECTOR_SIZE;
542: 
543:             // 确保擦除范围不超过分区大小
544:             if (sector_end > partition_->size)
545:             {
546:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Sector end (%u) exceeds partition size (%lu)", sector_end, partition_->size);
547:                 return false;
548:             }
549: 
550:             ESP_LOGD(TAG, "Erasing sector %u (offset: %u, size: %u)", current_sector, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
551:             esp_err_t err = esp_partition_erase_range(partition_, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
552:             if (err != ESP_OK)
553:             {
554:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to erase sector %u at offset %u: %s", current_sector, sector_start, esp_err_to_name(err));
555:                 return false;
556:             }
557: 
558:             current_sector++;
559:         }
560: 
561:         // 写入数据到分区
562:         esp_err_t err = esp_partition_write(partition_, total_written, buffer, ret);
563:         if (err != ESP_OK)
564:         {
565:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write to assets partition at offset %u: %s", total_written, esp_err_to_name(err));
566:             return false;
567:         }
568: 
569:         total_written += ret;
570:         recent_written += ret;
571: 
572:         // 计算进度和速度
573:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || total_written == content_length || ret == 0)
574:         {
575:             size_t progress = total_written * 100 / content_length;
576:             size_t speed = recent_written; // 每秒的字节数
577:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %u B/s, Sectors erased: %u",
578:                      progress, total_written, content_length, speed, current_sector);
579:             if (progress_callback)
580:             {
581:                 progress_callback(progress, speed);
582:             }
583:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
584:             recent_written = 0; // 重置最近写入的字节数
585:         }
586:     }
587: 
588:     http->Close();
589: 
590:     if (total_written != content_length)
591:     {
592:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Downloaded size (%u) does not match expected size (%u)", total_written, content_length);
593:         return false;
594:     }
595: 
596:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Assets download completed, total written: %u bytes, total sectors erased: %u",
597:              total_written, current_sector);
598: 
599:     // 重新初始化资源分区
600:     if (!InitializePartition())
601:     {
602:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to re-initialize assets partition");
603:         return false;
604:     }
605: 
606:     return true;
607: }

609: bool Assets::GetAssetData(const std::string &name, void *&ptr, size_t &size)
610: {
611:     auto asset = assets_.find(name);
612:     if (asset == assets_.end())
613:     {
614:         return false;
615:     }
616:     auto data = (const char *)(mmap_root_ + asset->second.offset);
617:     if (data[0] != 'Z' || data[1] != 'Z')
618:     {
619:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "The asset %s is not valid with magic %02x%02x", name.c_str(), data[0], data[1]);
620:         return false;
621:     }
622: 
623:     ptr = static_cast<void *>(const_cast<char *>(data + 2));
624:     size = asset->second.size;
625:     return true;
626: }
627: 
</file>
The function calculates a 16-bit checksum for a given data buffer by summing all byte values and masking the result to 16 bits. It is used to verify data integrity during asset downloads or storage operations. The checksum helps detect corruption or transmission errors in the asset files.

Returns true if the partition is successfully initialized and validated, false otherwise.

41: uint32_t Assets::CalculateChecksum(const char *data, uint32_t length)
42: {

377:                 cJSON *height = cJSON_GetObjectItem(layout_item, "height");

380:                 {

393:                 }

396:     }

403: bool Assets::Download(std::string url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> progress_callback)
404: {
405:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Downloading new version of assets from %s", url.c_str());
406: 
407:     // 取消当前资源分区的内存映射
408:     if (mmap_handle_ != 0)
409:     {
410:         esp_partition_munmap(mmap_handle_);
411:         mmap_handle_ = 0;
412:         mmap_root_ = nullptr;
413:     }
414:     checksum_valid_ = false;
415:     assets_.clear();
416: 
417:     // 下载新的资源文件
418:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
419:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
420: 
421:     if (!http->Open("GET", url))
422:     {
423:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
424:         return false;
425:     }
426: 
427:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
428:     {
429:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get assets, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
430:         return false;
431:     }
432: 
433:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
434:     if (content_length == 0)
435:     {
436:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
437:         return false;
438:     }
439: 
440:     if (content_length > partition_->size)
441:     {
442:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Assets file size (%u) is larger than partition size (%lu)", content_length, partition_->size);
443:         return false;
444:     }

446:     // 定义扇区大小为4KB（ESP32的标准扇区大小）
447:     const size_t SECTOR_SIZE = esp_partition_get_main_flash_sector_size();

449:     // 计算需要擦除的扇区数量
450:     size_t sectors_to_erase = (content_length + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE; // 向上取整
451:     size_t total_erase_size = sectors_to_erase * SECTOR_SIZE;
452: 
453:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Sector size: %u, content length: %u, sectors to erase: %u, total erase size: %u",
454:              SECTOR_SIZE, content_length, sectors_to_erase, total_erase_size);
455: 
456:     // 写入新的资源文件到分区，一边erase一边写入
457:     char buffer[512];
458:     size_t total_written = 0;
459:     size_t recent_written = 0;
460:     size_t current_sector = 0;
461:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
462: 
463:     while (true)
464:     {
465:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
466:         if (ret < 0)
467:         {
468:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
469:             return false;
470:         }
471: 
472:         if (ret == 0)
473:         {
474:             break;
475:         }
476: 
477:         // 检查是否需要擦除新的扇区
478:         size_t write_end_offset = total_written + ret;
479:         size_t needed_sectors = (write_end_offset + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE;
480: 
481:         // 擦除需要的新扇区
482:         while (current_sector < needed_sectors)
483:         {
484:             size_t sector_start = current_sector * SECTOR_SIZE;
485:             size_t sector_end = (current_sector + 1) * SECTOR_SIZE;
486: 
487:             // 确保擦除范围不超过分区大小
488:             if (sector_end > partition_->size)
489:             {
490:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Sector end (%u) exceeds partition size (%lu)", sector_end, partition_->size);
491:                 return false;
492:             }
493: 
494:             ESP_LOGD(TAG, "Erasing sector %u (offset: %u, size: %u)", current_sector, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
495:             esp_err_t err = esp_partition_erase_range(partition_, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
496:             if (err != ESP_OK)
497:             {
498:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to erase sector %u at offset %u: %s", current_sector, sector_start, esp_err_to_name(err));
499:                 return false;
500:             }
501: 
502:             current_sector++;
503:         }
504: 
505:         // 写入数据到分区
506:         esp_err_t err = esp_partition_write(partition_, total_written, buffer, ret);
507:         if (err != ESP_OK)
508:         {
509:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write to assets partition at offset %u: %s", total_written, esp_err_to_name(err));
510:             return false;
511:         }
512: 
513:         total_written += ret;
514:         recent_written += ret;
515: 
516:         // 计算进度和速度
517:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || total_written == content_length || ret == 0)
518:         {
519:             size_t progress = total_written * 100 / content_length;
520:             size_t speed = recent_written; // 每秒的字节数
521:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %u B/s, Sectors erased: %u",
522:                      progress, total_written, content_length, speed, current_sector);
523:             if (progress_callback)
524:             {
525:                 progress_callback(progress, speed);
526:             }
527:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
528:             recent_written = 0; // 重置最近写入的字节数
529:         }
530:     }
531: 
532:     http->Close();
533: 
534:     if (total_written != content_length)
535:     {
536:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Downloaded size (%u) does not match expected size (%u)", total_written, content_length);
537:         return false;
538:     }
539: 
540:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Assets download completed, total written: %u bytes, total sectors erased: %u",
541:              total_written, current_sector);
542: 
543:     // 重新初始化资源分区
544:     if (!InitializePartition())
545:     {
546:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to re-initialize assets partition");
547:         return false;
548:     }
549: 
550:     return true;
551: }

556:     if (asset == assets_.end())

561:     if (data[0] != 'Z' || data[1] != 'Z')

564:         return false;

567:     ptr = static_cast<void *>(const_cast<char *>(data + 2));

569:     return true;
570: }

The function initializes and validates an assets partition in flash memory, ensuring it exists, has sufficient space, and passes checksum verification. It maps the partition into memory and builds an index of assets with their sizes and offsets for efficient access. Returns true if the partition is successfully initialized and validated, false otherwise.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The core idea of initializing a partition and validating its contents is preserved.
- However, the method of validation differs significantly: ground truth uses checksum comparison with stored length/data, while generated code uses a magic number and per-asset validation.
- The generated code assumes a different data layout (magic number, asset count, asset entries, then checksum), which diverges from the ground truth's approach (file count, checksum, total length, followed by asset table).
- Error handling and logging are present but target different failure points.

Structure Similarity:
- Both functions follow a general flow of finding a partition, mapping it, performing validations, and building an asset index.
- Differences include:
  * Partition search parameters (generated uses DATA/SPIFFS subtype; ground truth uses ANY/ANY).
  * Memory mapping logic is similar but applied differently due to assumed data layouts.
  * Loop structures differ in purpose: ground truth reads a fixed-size table, while generated processes variable-sized entries.
  * Use of timers/checksums occurs at different stages and for different purposes.

Functional Completeness:
- Partial overlap in functionality: both initialize a partition, validate integrity, and populate an asset map.
- Missing elements in generated version:
  * No check against available flash memory pages (`spi_flash_mmap_get_free_pages`).
  * No timing measurement of checksum calculation.
  * Different asset metadata (adds width/height not present in ground truth).
  * Assumes presence of magic number and specific data layout not found in ground truth.
- Additional features in generated version that aren't in ground truth:
  * Per-entry validation and skipping of empty assets.
  * Magic number verification.
  * Detailed string-based asset naming with null termination enforcement.

Overall, while there is conceptual alignment, implementation details and assumptions about data format cause significant divergence in behavior and correctness relative to the reference.

qualitative.analysis: The generated code is a shallow stub that only logs messages and retrieves index.json, but fails to implement any actual logic for parsing JSON, validating versions, loading models, handling themes/fonts/emojis, or configuring display settings. It completely omits critical functionality like cJSON parsing, model loading via esp_srmodel, theme management (light/dark), emoji collections, skin configurations, and display updates. The structure lacks conditional compilation directives (#ifdef HAVE_LVGL), specific API calls (cJSON_GetObjectItem, srmodel_load), error handling for missing assets, and the complex object-oriented interactions present in the ground truth. While it maintains the same function signature and attempts to follow a high-level sequence (load index -> apply assets), it misses nearly all meaningful semantics and functional details required by the task.

results.description: The `Assets::Download` function downloads a new version of assets from a given URL and stores it in the device's flash memory partition. It handles HTTP communication, validates the content size against the partition size, and manages flash memory by erasing and writing data in sectors. The function also provides progress updates during the download process.

48:     return checksum & 0xFFFF;
49: }

51: bool Assets::InitializePartition()
52: {
53:     partition_valid_ = false;
54:     checksum_valid_ = false;
55:     assets_.clear();
56: 
57:     partition_ = esp_partition_find_first(ESP_PARTITION_TYPE_ANY, ESP_PARTITION_SUBTYPE_ANY, "assets");
58:     if (partition_ == nullptr)
59:     {
60:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No assets partition found");
61:         return false;
62:     }
63: 
64:     int free_pages = spi_flash_mmap_get_free_pages(SPI_FLASH_MMAP_DATA);
65:     uint32_t storage_size = free_pages * 64 * 1024;
66:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "The storage free size is %ld KB", storage_size / 1024);
67:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "The partition size is %ld KB", partition_->size / 1024);
68:     if (storage_size < partition_->size)
69:     {
70:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "The free size %ld KB is less than assets partition required %ld KB", storage_size / 1024, partition_->size / 1024);
71:         return false;
72:     }
73: 
74:     esp_err_t err = esp_partition_mmap(partition_, 0, partition_->size, ESP_PARTITION_MMAP_DATA, (const void **)&mmap_root_, &mmap_handle_);
75:     if (err != ESP_OK)
76:     {
77:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to mmap assets partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
78:         return false;
79:     }
80: 
81:     partition_valid_ = true;
82: 
83:     uint32_t stored_files = *(uint32_t *)(mmap_root_ + 0);
84:     uint32_t stored_chksum = *(uint32_t *)(mmap_root_ + 4);
85:     uint32_t stored_len = *(uint32_t *)(mmap_root_ + 8);
86: 
87:     if (stored_len > partition_->size - 12)
88:     {
89:         ESP_LOGD(TAG, "The stored_len (0x%lx) is greater than the partition size (0x%lx) - 12", stored_len, partition_->size);
90:         return false;
91:     }
92: 
93:     auto start_time = esp_timer_get_time();
94:     uint32_t calculated_checksum = CalculateChecksum(mmap_root_ + 12, stored_len);
95:     auto end_time = esp_timer_get_time();
96:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "The checksum calculation time is %d ms", int((end_time - start_time) / 1000));
97: 
98:     if (calculated_checksum != stored_chksum)
99:     {
100:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "The calculated checksum (0x%lx) does not match the stored checksum (0x%lx)", calculated_checksum, stored_chksum);
101:         return false;
102:     }
103: 
104:     checksum_valid_ = true;
105: 
106:     for (uint32_t i = 0; i < stored_files; i++)
107:     {
108:         auto item = (const mmap_assets_table *)(mmap_root_ + 12 + i * sizeof(mmap_assets_table));
109:         auto asset = Asset{
110:             .size = static_cast<size_t>(item->asset_size),
111:             .offset = static_cast<size_t>(12 + sizeof(mmap_assets_table) * stored_files + item->asset_offset)};
112:         assets_[item->asset_name] = asset;
113:     }
114:     return checksum_valid_;
115: }

469: bool Assets::GetAssetData(const std::string &name, void *&ptr, size_t &size)
470: {
471:     auto asset = assets_.find(name);
472:     if (asset == assets_.end())
473:     {
474:         return false;
475:     }
476:     auto data = (const char *)(mmap_root_ + asset->second.offset);
477:     if (data[0] != 'Z' || data[1] != 'Z')
478:     {
479:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "The asset %s is not valid with magic %02x%02x", name.c_str(), data[0], data[1]);
480:         return false;
481:     }
482: 
483:     ptr = static_cast<void *>(const_cast<char *>(data + 2));
484:     size = asset->second.size;
485:     return true;
486: }

</file>
The `Assets::Download` function downloads a new version of assets from a given URL and stores it in the device's flash memory partition. It handles HTTP communication, validates the content size against the partition size, and manages flash memory by erasing and writing data in sectors. The function also provides progress updates during the download process.

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code fails to implement the core logic required for downloading and writing assets to a partition. While it correctly identifies that the assets partition must be valid, it does not proceed with any actual download or write operations. It only logs a warning about requiring an HTTP client implementation and returns false, indicating incomplete functionality.

In contrast, the ground truth implements a complete workflow:
- Opening an HTTP connection using a custom Network/HTTP abstraction.
- Validating HTTP status codes and content length.
- Calculating and erasing flash sectors dynamically as data is downloaded.
- Writing incoming data in chunks to the ESP32 flash partition.
- Tracking progress and calling the provided callback at regular intervals.
- Reinitializing the partition after successful download.

Semantic correctness is low because the placeholder omits all meaningful behavior related to downloading, sector management, and progress tracking. Structure similarity is limited since the generated version lacks loops, error handling, HTTP interaction, or partition manipulation constructs found in the reference. Functional completeness is also poor as critical steps like HTTP communication, data writing, progress reporting, and post-download initialization are missing entirely.

results.query: complete the function GetAssetData(const std::string &, void *&, size_t &)()
results.description: The `Assets::GetAssetData` function retrieves the data pointer and size for a specified asset by name, validating its integrity through a magic number check. If the asset exists and is valid, it returns a pointer to the asset's data (excluding the header) and its size; otherwise, it returns false. This enables safe access to preloaded binary assets stored in memory.

110:             .size = static_cast<size_t>(item->asset_size),
111:             .offset = static_cast<size_t>(12 + sizeof(mmap_assets_table) * stored_files + item->asset_offset)};
112:         assets_[item->asset_name] = asset;
113:     }
114:     return checksum_valid_;
115: }

465: bool Assets::Download(std::string url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> progress_callback)
466: {
467:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Downloading new version of assets from %s", url.c_str());
468: 
469:     // 取消当前资源分区的内存映射
470:     if (mmap_handle_ != 0)
471:     {
472:         esp_partition_munmap(mmap_handle_);
473:         mmap_handle_ = 0;
474:         mmap_root_ = nullptr;
475:     }
476:     checksum_valid_ = false;
477:     assets_.clear();
478: 
479:     // 下载新的资源文件
480:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
481:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
482: 
483:     if (!http->Open("GET", url))
484:     {
485:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
486:         return false;
487:     }
488: 
489:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
490:     {
491:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get assets, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
492:         return false;
493:     }
494: 
495:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
496:     if (content_length == 0)
497:     {
498:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
499:         return false;
500:     }
501: 
502:     if (content_length > partition_->size)
503:     {
504:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Assets file size (%u) is larger than partition size (%lu)", content_length, partition_->size);
505:         return false;
506:     }
507: 
508:     // 定义扇区大小为4KB（ESP32的标准扇区大小）
509:     const size_t SECTOR_SIZE = esp_partition_get_main_flash_sector_size();
510: 
511:     // 计算需要擦除的扇区数量
512:     size_t sectors_to_erase = (content_length + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE; // 向上取整
513:     size_t total_erase_size = sectors_to_erase * SECTOR_SIZE;
514: 
515:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Sector size: %u, content length: %u, sectors to erase: %u, total erase size: %u",
516:              SECTOR_SIZE, content_length, sectors_to_erase, total_erase_size);
517: 
518:     // 写入新的资源文件到分区，一边erase一边写入
519:     char buffer[512];
520:     size_t total_written = 0;
521:     size_t recent_written = 0;
522:     size_t current_sector = 0;
523:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
524: 
525:     while (true)
526:     {
527:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
528:         if (ret < 0)
529:         {
530:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
531:             return false;
532:         }
533: 
534:         if (ret == 0)
535:         {
536:             break;
537:         }
538: 
539:         // 检查是否需要擦除新的扇区
540:         size_t write_end_offset = total_written + ret;
541:         size_t needed_sectors = (write_end_offset + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE;
542: 
543:         // 擦除需要的新扇区
544:         while (current_sector < needed_sectors)
545:         {
546:             size_t sector_start = current_sector * SECTOR_SIZE;
547:             size_t sector_end = (current_sector + 1) * SECTOR_SIZE;
548: 
549:             // 确保擦除范围不超过分区大小
550:             if (sector_end > partition_->size)
551:             {
552:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Sector end (%u) exceeds partition size (%lu)", sector_end, partition_->size);
553:                 return false;
554:             }
555: 
556:             ESP_LOGD(TAG, "Erasing sector %u (offset: %u, size: %u)", current_sector, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
557:             esp_err_t err = esp_partition_erase_range(partition_, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
558:             if (err != ESP_OK)
559:             {
560:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to erase sector %u at offset %u: %s", current_sector, sector_start, esp_err_to_name(err));
561:                 return false;
562:             }
563: 
564:             current_sector++;
565:         }
566: 
567:         // 写入数据到分区
568:         esp_err_t err = esp_partition_write(partition_, total_written, buffer, ret);
569:         if (err != ESP_OK)
570:         {
571:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write to assets partition at offset %u: %s", total_written, esp_err_to_name(err));
572:             return false;
573:         }
574: 
575:         total_written += ret;
576:         recent_written += ret;
577: 
578:         // 计算进度和速度
579:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || total_written == content_length || ret == 0)
580:         {
581:             size_t progress = total_written * 100 / content_length;
582:             size_t speed = recent_written; // 每秒的字节数
583:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %u B/s, Sectors erased: %u",
584:                      progress, total_written, content_length, speed, current_sector);
585:             if (progress_callback)
586:             {
587:                 progress_callback(progress, speed);
588:             }
589:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
590:             recent_written = 0; // 重置最近写入的字节数
591:         }
592:     }
593: 
594:     http->Close();
595: 
596:     if (total_written != content_length)
597:     {
598:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Downloaded size (%u) does not match expected size (%u)", total_written, content_length);
599:         return false;
600:     }
601: 
602:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Assets download completed, total written: %u bytes, total sectors erased: %u",
603:              total_written, current_sector);
604: 
605:     // 重新初始化资源分区
606:     if (!InitializePartition())
607:     {
608:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to re-initialize assets partition");
609:         return false;
610:     }
611: 
612:     return true;
613: }

The `Assets::GetAssetData` function retrieves the data pointer and size for a specified asset by name, validating its integrity through a magic number check. If the asset exists and is valid, it returns a pointer to the asset's data (excluding the header) and its size; otherwise, it returns false. This enables safe access to preloaded binary assets stored in memory.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (60/100):
The generated code attempts to implement a similar functionality but differs significantly in logic details:
- Both functions look up an asset by name using `assets_.find()`.
- However, the magic number check differs: Ground truth checks for two bytes 'Z','Z', while generated code expects a 4-byte magic "ASSE".
- The way data pointer is adjusted also differs; ground truth skips 2 bytes, whereas generated code skips 4 bytes.
- Generated code includes additional validation (`partition_valid_`, `checksum_valid_`, bounds checking) not present in the ground truth.

Structure Similarity (75/100):
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Control flow follows a similar pattern: early returns on failure conditions, then processing and returning success.
- Uses same logging macro `ESP_LOGE` with tag.
- Differences include extra validation steps and different approaches to handling headers/magic numbers.

Functional Completeness (55/100):
- Core functionality of retrieving asset data by name is covered.
- Missing exact behavior regarding how header/magic bytes are handled (byte count and values).
- Includes additional validations that may be outside the required scope.
- Incorrectly assumes a 4-byte magic header instead of 2-byte one from ground truth.
- Adjusts output size differently than the reference implementation.

31: bool DeviceStateMachine::IsValidTransition(DeviceState from, DeviceState to) const
32: {
33:     // Allow transition to the same state (no-op)
34:     if (from == to)
35:     {
36:         return true;
37:     }
38: 
39:     // Define valid state transitions based on the state diagram
40:     switch (from)
41:     {
42:     case kDeviceStateUnknown:
43:         // Can only go to starting
44:         return to == kDeviceStateStarting;
45: 
46:     case kDeviceStateStarting:
47:         // Can go to wifi configuring or activating
48:         return to == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring ||
49:                to == kDeviceStateActivating;
50: 
51:     case kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring:
52:         // Can go to activating (after wifi connected) or audio testing
53:         return to == kDeviceStateActivating ||
54:                to == kDeviceStateAudioTesting;
55: 
56:     case kDeviceStateAudioTesting:
57:         // Can go back to wifi configuring
58:         return to == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring;

109: bool DeviceStateMachine::TransitionTo(DeviceState new_state)
110: {

112:

114:     if (old_state == new_state)

124:         return false;
125:     }

155: void DeviceStateMachine::NotifyStateChange(DeviceState old_state, DeviceState new_state)
156: {

160:         callbacks_copy.reserve(listeners_.size());
161:         for (const auto &[id, cb] : listeners_)

164:         }
165:     }
166:

168:     {
169:         cb(old_state, new_state);
170:     }
171: }

It validates whether a requested state change is allowed, performs the transition if valid, and notifies registered observers of the change. This ensures controlled and predictable behavior as the device moves between defined states such as idle, active, or error conditions.

All function signatures, control flow constructs, API calls (including ESP logging macros), and method invocations match perfectly.

There are no missing features or extraneous additions.

114: bool DeviceStateMachine::TransitionTo(DeviceState new_state)

117:

122:     }

131:

135:              GetStateName(old_state), GetStateName(new_state));
136:

138:     NotifyStateChange(old_state, new_state);
139:     return true;

143: {

154: void DeviceStateMachine::NotifyStateChange(DeviceState old_state, DeviceState new_state)
155: {
156:     std::vector<StateCallback> callbacks_copy;
157:     {
158:         std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
159:         callbacks_copy.reserve(listeners_.size());
160:         for (const auto &[id, cb] : listeners_)
161:         {
162:             callbacks_copy.push_back(cb);
163:         }
164:     }
165: 
166:     for (const auto &cb : callbacks_copy)
167:     {
168:         cb(old_state, new_state);
169:     }
170: }

The `RemoveStateChangeListener` function removes a previously registered state change listener from the device's state machine based on its unique identifier. It ensures thread-safe access to the listener list by using a mutex lock during the removal process. This allows dynamic management of components that need to respond to device state transitions.

36: bool DeviceStateMachine::IsValidTransition(DeviceState from, DeviceState to) const
37: {
38:     // Allow transition to the same state (no-op)
39:     if (from == to)
40:     {
41:         return true;
42:     }
43: 
44:     // Define valid state transitions based on the state diagram
45:     switch (from)
46:     {
47:     case kDeviceStateUnknown:
48:         // Can only go to starting
49:         return to == kDeviceStateStarting;
50: 
51:     case kDeviceStateStarting:
52:         // Can go to wifi configuring or activating
53:         return to == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring ||
54:                to == kDeviceStateActivating;
55: 
56:     case kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring:
57:         // Can go to activating (after wifi connected) or audio testing
58:         return to == kDeviceStateActivating ||
59:                to == kDeviceStateAudioTesting;
60: 
61:     case kDeviceStateAudioTesting:
62:         // Can go back to wifi configuring
63:         return to == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring;
64: 
65:     case kDeviceStateActivating:
66:         // Can go to upgrading, idle, or back to wifi configuring (on error)
67:         return to == kDeviceStateUpgrading ||
68:                to == kDeviceStateIdle ||
69:                to == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring;
70: 
71:     case kDeviceStateUpgrading:
72:         // Can go to idle (upgrade failed) or activating
73:         return to == kDeviceStateIdle ||
74:                to == kDeviceStateActivating;
75: 
76:     case kDeviceStateIdle:
77:         // Can go to connecting, listening (manual mode), speaking, activating, upgrading, or wifi configuring
78:         return to == kDeviceStateConnecting ||
79:                to == kDeviceStateListening ||
80:                to == kDeviceStateSpeaking ||
81:                to == kDeviceStateActivating ||
82:                to == kDeviceStateUpgrading ||
83:                to == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring;
84: 
85:     case kDeviceStateConnecting:
86:         // Can go to idle (failed) or listening (success)
87:         return to == kDeviceStateIdle ||
88:                to == kDeviceStateListening;
89: 
90:     case kDeviceStateListening:
91:         // Can go to speaking or idle
92:         return to == kDeviceStateSpeaking ||
93:                to == kDeviceStateIdle;
94: 
95:     case kDeviceStateSpeaking:
96:         // Can go to listening or idle
97:         return to == kDeviceStateListening ||
98:                to == kDeviceStateIdle;
99: 
100:     case kDeviceStateFatalError:
101:         // Cannot transition out of fatal error
102:         return false;
103: 
104:     default:
105:         return false;
106:     }
107: }

The `NotifyStateChange` function notifies all registered listeners of a device state transition by invoking their callback functions with the old and new states. It ensures thread-safe execution by copying the list of callbacks under a mutex lock before invoking them. This allows decoupled components to react to device state changes without direct dependencies.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (85): The generated code captures the core functionality of notifying state change listeners. Both implementations lock the mutex, create a copy of listeners to avoid modification during iteration, and call each callback with old/new state parameters. However, there are some semantic differences: the generated code uses try-catch to silently handle exceptions while the ground truth lets exceptions propagate, and the generated code copies the entire listeners_ container while ground truth extracts only the callback functions.

Structure Similarity (70): The function signature matches perfectly, but the structural approach differs. Ground truth uses explicit vector copying with reserve() for efficiency and iterates over a map's key-value pairs using structured bindings [id, cb]. Generated code directly copies the listeners_ container and accesses the second element (.second) explicitly. The locking scope also differs - ground truth releases the lock earlier by using a separate scope block.

Functional Completeness (80): The generated code covers the main functionality of iterating through listeners and calling callbacks. It includes additional exception handling which could be seen as extra functionality. However, it misses the performance optimization of using reserve() to pre-allocate vector space. The core notification logic is present, but the implementation details and error handling approaches differ from the ground truth.

This ensures each device can be uniquely identified during over-the-air update processes.

302: }

304: bool Ota::Upgrade(const std::string &firmware_url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
305: {
306:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Upgrading firmware from %s", firmware_url.c_str());
307:     esp_ota_handle_t update_handle = 0;
308:     auto update_partition = esp_ota_get_next_update_partition(NULL);
309:     if (update_partition == NULL)
310:     {
311:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get update partition");
312:         return false;
313:     }
314: 
315:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Writing to partition %s at offset 0x%lx", update_partition->label, update_partition->address);
316:     bool image_header_checked = false;
317:     std::string image_header;
318: 
319:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
320:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
321:     if (!http->Open("GET", firmware_url))
322:     {
323:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
324:         return false;
325:     }
326: 
327:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
328:     {
329:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get firmware, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
330:         return false;
331:     }
332: 
333:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
334:     if (content_length == 0)
335:     {
336:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
337:         return false;
338:     }
339: 
340:     char buffer[512];
341:     size_t total_read = 0, recent_read = 0;
342:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
343:     while (true)
344:     {
345:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
346:         if (ret < 0)
347:         {
348:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
349:             return false;
350:         }
351: 
352:         // Calculate speed and progress every second
353:         recent_read += ret;
354:         total_read += ret;
355:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || ret == 0)
356:         {
357:             size_t progress = total_read * 100 / content_length;
358:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %uB/s", progress, total_read, content_length, recent_read);
359:             if (callback)
360:             {
361:                 callback(progress, recent_read);
362:             }
363:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
364:             recent_read = 0;
365:         }
366: 
367:         if (ret == 0)
368:         {
369:             break;
370:         }
371: 
372:         if (!image_header_checked)
373:         {
374:             image_header.append(buffer, ret);
375:             if (image_header.size() >= sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t) + sizeof(esp_app_desc_t))
376:             {
377:                 esp_app_desc_t new_app_info;
378:                 memcpy(&new_app_info, image_header.data() + sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t), sizeof(esp_app_desc_t));
379: 
380:                 auto current_version = esp_app_get_description()->version;
381:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s, New version: %s", current_version, new_app_info.version);
382: 
383:                 if (esp_ota_begin(update_partition, OTA_WITH_SEQUENTIAL_WRITES, &update_handle))
384:                 {
385:                     esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
386:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to begin OTA");
387:                     return false;
388:                 }
389: 
390:                 image_header_checked = true;
391:                 std::string().swap(image_header);
392:             }
393:         }
394:         auto err = esp_ota_write(update_handle, buffer, ret);
395:         if (err != ESP_OK)
396:         {
397:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write OTA data: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
398:             esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
399:             return false;
400:         }
401:     }
402:     http->Close();
403: 
404:     esp_err_t err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
405:     if (err != ESP_OK)
406:     {
407:         if (err == ESP_ERR_OTA_VALIDATE_FAILED)
408:         {
409:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Image validation failed, image is corrupted");
410:         }
411:         else
412:         {
413:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to end OTA: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
414:         }
415:         return false;
416:     }
417: 
418:     err = esp_ota_set_boot_partition(update_partition);
419:     if (err != ESP_OK)
420:     {
421:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to set boot partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
422:         return false;
423:     }
424: 
425:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful");
426:     return true;
427: }
428: 
429: bool Ota::StartUpgrade(std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
430: {
431:     return Upgrade(firmware_url_, callback);
432: }

434: std::vector<int> Ota::ParseVersion(const std::string &version)
435: {
436:     std::vector<int> versionNumbers;
437:     std::stringstream ss(version);
438:     std::string segment;
439: 
440:     while (std::getline(ss, segment, '.'))
441:     {
442:         versionNumbers.push_back(std::stoi(segment));
443:     }
444: 
445:     return versionNumbers;
446: }
447: 
448: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
449: {
450:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
451:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
452: 
453:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
454:     {
455:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
456:         {
457:             return true;
458:         }
459:         else if (newer[i] < current[i])
460:         {
461:             return false;
462:         }
463:     }
464: 
465:     return newer.size() > current.size();
466: }

468: std::string Ota::GetActivationPayload()
469: {
470:     if (!has_serial_number_)
471:     {
472:         return "{}";
473:     }
474: 
475:     std::string hmac_hex;
476: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
477:     uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
478: 
479:     // 使用Key0计算HMAC
480:     esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
481:     if (ret != ESP_OK)
482:     {
483:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
484:         return "{}";
485:     }
486: 
487:     for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
488:     {
489:         char buffer[3];
490:         sprintf(buffer, "%02x", hmac_result[i]);
491:         hmac_hex += buffer;
492:     }
493: #endif
494: 
495:     cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
496:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
497:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
498:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
499:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());
500:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload);
501:     std::string json(json_str);
502:     cJSON_free(json_str);
503:     cJSON_Delete(payload);
504: 
505:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
506:     return json;
507: }
508: 
509: esp_err_t Ota::Activate()
510: {
511:     if (!has_activation_challenge_)
512:     {
513:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No activation challenge found");
514:         return ESP_FAIL;
515:     }
516: 
517:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
518:     if (url.back() != '/')
519:     {
520:         url += "/activate";
521:     }
522:     else
523:     {
524:         url += "activate";
525:     }
526: 
527:     auto http = SetupHttp();
528: 
529:     std::string data = GetActivationPayload();
530:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
531: 
532:     if (!http->Open("POST", url))
533:     {
534:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
535:         return ESP_FAIL;
536:     }
537: 
538:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
539:     if (status_code == 202)
540:     {
541:         return ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT;
542:     }
543:     if (status_code != 200)
544:     {
545:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to activate, code: %d, body: %s", status_code, http->ReadAll().c_str());
546:         return ESP_FAIL;
547:     }
548: 
549:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation successful");
550:     return ESP_OK;
551: }

</file>
The OTA constructor initializes the device's serial number by reading it from the ESP32's eFuse user data block. If the read operation is successful and the serial number is not empty, it stores the value for use in firmware updates and device identification. This ensures each device can be uniquely identified during over-the-air update processes.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to read serial number data from eFuse, which aligns with the intent of the ground truth.
- However, there are critical semantic differences:
  * The generated code assumes a fixed size of 12 bytes for the serial number, while the ground truth uses 32 bytes and checks if the first byte is zero to determine validity.
  * The generated code converts the binary data into a hexadecimal string representation, whereas the ground truth treats it as raw character data.
  * The logging behavior differs significantly; the ground truth does not include any logging, while the generated code includes extensive logging via ESP_LOG macros.

Structure Similarity:
- Both codes use constructors for the Ota class and attempt to read from ESP_EFUSE_USER_DATA using esp_efuse_read_field_blob().
- Control flow structures differ: the ground truth wraps the functionality inside #ifdef guards and performs conditional assignment based on data content, while the generated code unconditionally processes the data after reading.
- Function signature matches in name but lacks initialization list or other potential members that might be present in a full implementation.

Functional Completeness:
- The generated code covers basic functionality of reading eFuse data and assigning it to member variables.
- It introduces additional features such as hex conversion and logging that are not part of the ground truth.
- Missing elements include:
  * Conditional compilation guard (#ifdef ESP_EFUSE_BLOCK_USR_DATA)
  * Proper handling of empty/invalid serial numbers beyond just checking string emptiness
  * Exact data length and interpretation matching the ground truth logic

Overall, the generated code shows an understanding of the task but deviates in several important ways regarding data handling, assumptions about data size, and added functionalities not present in the reference.

results.description: The function retrieves the OTA version check URL from the Wi-Fi settings, falling back to a default configuration value if no URL is set. It ensures the device can always access the correct endpoint for firmware version checks.

360: 
361:         // Calculate speed and progress every second
362:         recent_read += ret;
363:         total_read += ret;
364:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || ret == 0)
365:         {
366:             size_t progress = total_read * 100 / content_length;
367:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %uB/s", progress, total_read, content_length, recent_read);
368:             if (callback)
369:             {
370:                 callback(progress, recent_read);
371:             }
372:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
373:             recent_read = 0;
374:         }
375: 
376:         if (ret == 0)
377:         {
378:             break;
379:         }
380: 
381:         if (!image_header_checked)
382:         {
383:             image_header.append(buffer, ret);
384:             if (image_header.size() >= sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t) + sizeof(esp_app_desc_t))
385:             {
386:                 esp_app_desc_t new_app_info;
387:                 memcpy(&new_app_info, image_header.data() + sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t), sizeof(esp_app_desc_t));
388: 
389:                 auto current_version = esp_app_get_description()->version;
390:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s, New version: %s", current_version, new_app_info.version);
391: 
392:                 if (esp_ota_begin(update_partition, OTA_WITH_SEQUENTIAL_WRITES, &update_handle))
393:                 {
394:                     esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
395:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to begin OTA");
396:                     return false;
397:                 }
398: 
399:                 image_header_checked = true;
400:                 std::string().swap(image_header);
401:             }
402:         }
403:         auto err = esp_ota_write(update_handle, buffer, ret);
404:         if (err != ESP_OK)
405:         {
406:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write OTA data: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
407:             esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
408:             return false;
409:         }
410:     }
411:     http->Close();
412: 
413:     esp_err_t err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
414:     if (err != ESP_OK)
415:     {
416:         if (err == ESP_ERR_OTA_VALIDATE_FAILED)
417:         {
418:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Image validation failed, image is corrupted");
419:         }
420:         else
421:         {
422:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to end OTA: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
423:         }
424:         return false;
425:     }
426: 
427:     err = esp_ota_set_boot_partition(update_partition);
428:     if (err != ESP_OK)
429:     {
430:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to set boot partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
431:         return false;
432:     }
433: 
434:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful");
435:     return true;
436: }

443: std::vector<int> Ota::ParseVersion(const std::string &version)
444: {
445:     std::vector<int> versionNumbers;
446:     std::stringstream ss(version);
447:     std::string segment;
448: 
449:     while (std::getline(ss, segment, '.'))
450:     {
451:         versionNumbers.push_back(std::stoi(segment));
452:     }
453: 
454:     return versionNumbers;
455: }

457: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
458: {
459:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
460:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
461: 
462:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
463:     {
464:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
465:         {
466:             return true;
467:         }
468:         else if (newer[i] < current[i])
469:         {
470:             return false;
471:         }
472:     }
473: 
474:     return newer.size() > current.size();
475: }

477: std::string Ota::GetActivationPayload()
478: {
479:     if (!has_serial_number_)
480:     {
481:         return "{}";
482:     }
483: 
484:     std::string hmac_hex;
485: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
486:     uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
487: 
488:     // 使用Key0计算HMAC
489:     esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
490:     if (ret != ESP_OK)
491:     {
492:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
493:         return "{}";
494:     }
495: 
496:     for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
497:     {
498:         char buffer[3];
499:         sprintf(buffer, "%02x", hmac_result[i]);
500:         hmac_hex += buffer;
501:     }
502: #endif
503: 
504:     cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
505:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
506:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
507:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
508:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());
509:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload);
510:     std::string json(json_str);
511:     cJSON_free(json_str);
512:     cJSON_Delete(payload);
513: 
514:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
515:     return json;
516: }

518: esp_err_t Ota::Activate()
519: {
520:     if (!has_activation_challenge_)
521:     {
522:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No activation challenge found");
523:         return ESP_FAIL;
524:     }
525: 
526:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
527:     if (url.back() != '/')
528:     {
529:         url += "/activate";
530:     }
531:     else
532:     {
533:         url += "activate";
534:     }
535: 
536:     auto http = SetupHttp();
537: 
538:     std::string data = GetActivationPayload();
539:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
540: 
541:     if (!http->Open("POST", url))
542:     {
543:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
544:         return ESP_FAIL;
545:     }
546: 
547:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
548:     if (status_code == 202)
549:     {
550:         return ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT;
551:     }
552:     if (status_code != 200)
553:     {
554:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to activate, code: %d, body: %s", status_code, http->ReadAll().c_str());
555:         return ESP_FAIL;
556:     }
557: 
558:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation successful");
559:     return ESP_OK;
560: }

The function retrieves the OTA version check URL from the Wi-Fi settings, falling back to a default configuration value if no URL is set. It ensures the device can always access the correct endpoint for firmware version checks.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (60/100): The generated code follows a similar logical flow - getting settings, retrieving a URL, and providing a fallback. However, there are significant semantic differences: 1) Uses "wifi" settings with read-write mode (true) vs read-only mode (false) in ground truth, 2) Looks for "ota_check_version_url" setting key vs "ota_url", 3) Uses a hardcoded default URL vs CONFIG_OTA_URL constant. These differences change the intended behavior.

Structure Similarity (45/100): Major structural differences include: 1) Function signature is correct but formatting differs slightly, 2) Different constructor parameters for Settings object (true vs false), 3) Different setting key names, 4) Hardcoded string vs preprocessor constant, 5) Comment style and verbosity differ significantly. The overall control flow pattern is maintained but implementation details vary considerably.

Functional Completeness (55/100): The core functionality of retrieving a URL with fallback is present, but key specifics are missing or incorrect: 1) Wrong settings access mode, 2) Incorrect setting key name, 3) Missing use of CONFIG_OTA_URL constant, 4) Settings scope appears different. The basic purpose is achieved but with potentially different runtime behavior than intended.

results.description: The `Ota::SetupHttp` function configures and returns an HTTP client instance tailored for OTA (Over-The-Air) firmware updates. It sets essential headers including device identification, user agent, language, content type, and conditional serial number based on device provisioning status. This prepares the HTTP client for secure and context-aware communication with the OTA update server.

549:     return ESP_OK;

The `Ota::SetupHttp` function configures and returns an HTTP client instance tailored for OTA (Over-The-Air) firmware updates. It sets essential headers including device identification, user agent, language, content type, and conditional serial number based on device provisioning status. This prepares the HTTP client for secure and context-aware communication with the OTA update server.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The overall goal of setting up an HTTP client with appropriate headers is preserved.
- However, there are significant differences in how device identification and metadata are handled.
- The use of `Board::GetInstance()` is retained but applied differently.
- Key identifiers like "Device-Id", "Client-Id", and activation version logic based on serial number presence are missing or incorrectly implemented.
- Logging behavior from the ground truth is completely omitted in the generated code.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature differs; the ground truth returns a network-created Http instance while the generated code creates a generic unique_ptr<Http>.
- Control flow shows some similarity in header assignment sequence, but diverges significantly in implementation details.
- APIs used for retrieving system information differ (e.g., `SystemInfo::GetUserAgent()` vs. `esp_app_get_description()`).
- Use of conditional logic for serial number handling exists in both but with different header names and logging behavior.

Functional Completeness:
- Several critical headers from the ground truth are either renamed (`X-Device-Id` instead of `Device-Id`, `X-Serial-Number` instead of `Serial-Number`) or entirely absent (`Activation-Version`, `Client-Id`).
- Language configuration uses a different method than the ground truth's `Lang::CODE`.
- Missing integration with networking layer to create the Http object as done in the ground truth.
- No support for dynamic User-Agent composition using MAC address or board UUID.

results.description: The `Ota::CheckVersion()` function checks for available firmware updates by sending a request to a remote server and parsing the JSON response to determine if a newer version exists. It also handles activation-related data and MQTT configuration updates if present in the response. The function returns an error code indicating success or failure of the operation.

The `Ota::CheckVersion()` function checks for available firmware updates by sending a request to a remote server and parsing the JSON response to determine if a newer version exists. It also handles activation-related data and MQTT configuration updates if present in the response. The function returns an error code indicating success or failure of the operation.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to implement version checking via HTTP and parses a JSON response.
- However, it lacks many important features such as POST request handling based on system info, activation code parsing, WebSocket config updates, server time synchronization, and proper error logging with codes.
- It also assumes fixed paths like "/version" which may not align with `GetCheckVersionUrl()` logic from the ground truth.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches (`esp_err_t Ota::CheckVersion()`).
- Basic control flow is somewhat similar: setup HTTP, make request, read response, parse JSON.
- But key structural elements differ significantly: missing use of Board instance, no conditional GET/POST logic, different JSON field names ("download_url" vs "url"), simplified error handling without returning specific error codes.
- Uses hardcoded string appending instead of robust URL construction.

Functional Completeness:
- Misses critical functionality including:
  * Conditional GET/POST requests depending on system data availability.
  * Parsing and applying MQTT configurations dynamically using iteration over fields.
  * Handling WebSocket configurations.
  * Server time update using `settimeofday`.
  * Activation code and message handling.
  * Force firmware upgrade flag.
  * Proper status code propagation (uses ESP_FAIL everywhere rather than actual codes).
- Only partially implements firmware version comparison and basic challenge handling.
- Lacks detailed logging and structured error reporting that’s present in the reference implementation.

results.description: The `Ota::MarkCurrentVersionValid` function validates the currently running firmware partition by marking it as valid if it is in a pending verification state. It skips validation for the factory partition and logs the process for debugging purposes. This ensures that the device will boot into the new firmware on subsequent startups unless a rollback is explicitly triggered.
