48: bool MqttProtocol::StartMqttClient(bool report_error)
49: {
50:     if (mqtt_ != nullptr)
51:     {
52:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Mqtt client already started");
53:         mqtt_.reset();
54:     }
55: 
56:     Settings settings("mqtt", false);
57:     auto endpoint = settings.GetString("endpoint");
58:     auto client_id = settings.GetString("client_id");
59:     auto username = settings.GetString("username");
60:     auto password = settings.GetString("password");
61:     int keepalive_interval = settings.GetInt("keepalive", 240);
62:     publish_topic_ = settings.GetString("publish_topic");
63: 
64:     if (endpoint.empty())
65:     {
66:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "MQTT endpoint is not specified");
67:         if (report_error)
68:         {
69:             SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_NOT_FOUND);
70:         }
71:         return false;
72:     }
73: 
74:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
75:     mqtt_ = network->CreateMqtt(0);
76:     mqtt_->SetKeepAlive(keepalive_interval);
77: 
78:     mqtt_->OnDisconnected([this]()
79:                           {
80:         if (on_disconnected_ != nullptr) {
81:             on_disconnected_();
82:         }
83:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "MQTT disconnected, schedule reconnect in %d seconds", MQTT_RECONNECT_INTERVAL_MS / 1000);
84:         esp_timer_start_once(reconnect_timer_, MQTT_RECONNECT_INTERVAL_MS * 1000); });
85: 
86:     mqtt_->OnConnected([this]()
87:                        {
88:         if (on_connected_ != nullptr) {
89:             on_connected_();
90:         }
91:         esp_timer_stop(reconnect_timer_); });
92: 
93:     mqtt_->OnMessage([this](const std::string &topic, const std::string &payload)
94:                      {
95:         cJSON* root = cJSON_Parse(payload.c_str());
96:         if (root == nullptr) {
97:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to parse json message %s", payload.c_str());
98:             return;
99:         }
100:         cJSON* type = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "type");
101:         if (!cJSON_IsString(type)) {
102:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Message type is invalid");
103:             cJSON_Delete(root);
104:             return;
105:         }
106: 
107:         if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "hello") == 0) {
108:             ParseServerHello(root);
109:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "goodbye") == 0) {
110:             auto session_id = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "session_id");
111:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Received goodbye message, session_id: %s", session_id ? session_id->valuestring : "null");
112:             if (session_id == nullptr || session_id_ == session_id->valuestring) {
113:                 auto alive = alive_;  // Capture alive flag
114:                 Application::GetInstance().Schedule([this, alive]() {
115:                     if (*alive) {
116:                         CloseAudioChannel();
117:                     }
118:                 });
119:             }
120:         } else if (on_incoming_json_ != nullptr) {
121:             on_incoming_json_(root);
122:         }
123:         cJSON_Delete(root);
124:         last_incoming_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now(); });
125: 
126:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Connecting to endpoint %s", endpoint.c_str());
127:     std::string broker_address;
128:     int broker_port = 8883;
129:     size_t pos = endpoint.find(':');
130:     if (pos != std::string::npos)
131:     {
132:         broker_address = endpoint.substr(0, pos);
133:         broker_port = std::stoi(endpoint.substr(pos + 1));
134:     }
135:     else
136:     {
137:         broker_address = endpoint;
138:     }
139:     if (!mqtt_->Connect(broker_address, broker_port, client_id, username, password))
140:     {
141:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to connect to endpoint, code=%d", mqtt_->GetLastError());
142:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_NOT_CONNECTED);
143:         return false;
144:     }
145: 
146:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Connected to endpoint");
147:     return true;
148: }

165: bool MqttProtocol::SendAudio(std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> packet)
166: {
167:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(channel_mutex_);
168:     if (udp_ == nullptr)
169:     {
170:         return false;
171:     }
172: 
173:     std::string nonce(aes_nonce_);
174:     *(uint16_t *)&nonce[2] = htons(packet->payload.size());
175:     *(uint32_t *)&nonce[8] = htonl(packet->timestamp);
176:     *(uint32_t *)&nonce[12] = htonl(++local_sequence_);
177: 
178:     std::string encrypted;
179:     encrypted.resize(aes_nonce_.size() + packet->payload.size());
180:     memcpy(encrypted.data(), nonce.data(), nonce.size());
181: 
182:     size_t nc_off = 0;
183:     uint8_t stream_block[16] = {0};
184:     if (mbedtls_aes_crypt_ctr(&aes_ctx_, packet->payload.size(), &nc_off, (uint8_t *)nonce.c_str(), stream_block,
185:                               (uint8_t *)packet->payload.data(), (uint8_t *)&encrypted[nonce.size()]) != 0)
186:     {
187:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to encrypt audio data");
188:         return false;
189:     }
190: 
191:     return udp_->Send(encrypted) > 0;
192: }

194: void MqttProtocol::CloseAudioChannel()
195: {
196:     {
197:         std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(channel_mutex_);
198:         udp_.reset();
199:     }
200: 
201:     std::string message = "{";
202:     message += "\"session_id\":\"" + session_id_ + "\",";
203:     message += "\"type\":\"goodbye\"";
204:     message += "}";
205:     SendText(message);
206: 
207:     if (on_audio_channel_closed_ != nullptr)
208:     {
209:         on_audio_channel_closed_();
210:     }
211: }

213: bool MqttProtocol::OpenAudioChannel()
214: {
215:     if (mqtt_ == nullptr || !mqtt_->IsConnected())
216:     {
217:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "MQTT is not connected, try to connect now");
218:         if (!StartMqttClient(true))
219:         {
220:             return false;
221:         }
222:     }
223: 
224:     error_occurred_ = false;
225:     session_id_ = "";
226:     xEventGroupClearBits(event_group_handle_, MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT);
227: 
228:     auto message = GetHelloMessage();
229:     if (!SendText(message))
230:     {
231:         return false;
232:     }
233: 
234:     // 等待服务器响应
235:     EventBits_t bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_handle_, MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
236:     if (!(bits & MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT))
237:     {
238:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to receive server hello");
239:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_TIMEOUT);
240:         return false;
241:     }
242: 
243:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(channel_mutex_);
244:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
245:     udp_ = network->CreateUdp(2);
246:     udp_->OnMessage([this](const std::string &data)
247:                     {
248:         /*
249:          * UDP Encrypted OPUS Packet Format:
250:          * |type 1u|flags 1u|payload_len 2u|ssrc 4u|timestamp 4u|sequence 4u|
251:          * |payload payload_len|
252:          */
253:         if (data.size() < sizeof(aes_nonce_)) {
254:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid audio packet size: %u", data.size());
255:             return;
256:         }
257:         if (data[0] != 0x01) {
258:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid audio packet type: %x", data[0]);
259:             return;
260:         }
261:         uint32_t timestamp = ntohl(*(uint32_t*)&data[8]);
262:         uint32_t sequence = ntohl(*(uint32_t*)&data[12]);
263:         if (sequence < remote_sequence_) {
264:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Received audio packet with old sequence: %lu, expected: %lu", sequence, remote_sequence_);
265:             return;
266:         }
267:         if (sequence != remote_sequence_ + 1) {
268:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Received audio packet with wrong sequence: %lu, expected: %lu", sequence, remote_sequence_ + 1);
269:         }
270: 
271:         size_t decrypted_size = data.size() - aes_nonce_.size();
272:         size_t nc_off = 0;
273:         uint8_t stream_block[16] = {0};
274:         auto nonce = (uint8_t*)data.data();
275:         auto encrypted = (uint8_t*)data.data() + aes_nonce_.size();
276:         auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
277:         packet->sample_rate = server_sample_rate_;
278:         packet->frame_duration = server_frame_duration_;
279:         packet->timestamp = timestamp;
280:         packet->payload.resize(decrypted_size);
281:         int ret = mbedtls_aes_crypt_ctr(&aes_ctx_, decrypted_size, &nc_off, nonce, stream_block, encrypted, (uint8_t*)packet->payload.data());
282:         if (ret != 0) {
283:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to decrypt audio data, ret: %d", ret);
284:             return;
285:         }
286:         if (on_incoming_audio_ != nullptr) {
287:             on_incoming_audio_(std::move(packet));
288:         }
289:         remote_sequence_ = sequence;
290:         last_incoming_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now(); });
291: 
292:     udp_->Connect(udp_server_, udp_port_);
293: 
294:     if (on_audio_channel_opened_ != nullptr)
295:     {
296:         on_audio_channel_opened_();
297:     }
298:     return true;
299: }

301: std::string MqttProtocol::GetHelloMessage()
302: {
303:     // 发送 hello 消息申请 UDP 通道
304:     cJSON *root = cJSON_CreateObject();
305:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "type", "hello");
306:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(root, "version", 3);
307:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "transport", "udp");
308:     cJSON *features = cJSON_CreateObject();
309: #if CONFIG_USE_SERVER_AEC
310:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "aec", true);
311: #endif
312:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "mcp", true);
313:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "features", features);
314:     cJSON *audio_params = cJSON_CreateObject();
315:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(audio_params, "format", "opus");
316:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "sample_rate", 16000);
317:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "channels", 1);
318:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "frame_duration", OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS);
319:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "audio_params", audio_params);
320:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root);
321:     std::string message(json_str);
322:     cJSON_free(json_str);
323:     cJSON_Delete(root);
324:     return message;
325: }
326: 
327: void MqttProtocol::ParseServerHello(const cJSON *root)
328: {
329:     auto transport = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "transport");
330:     if (transport == nullptr || strcmp(transport->valuestring, "udp") != 0)
331:     {
332:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Unsupported transport: %s", transport->valuestring);
333:         return;
334:     }
335: 
336:     auto session_id = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "session_id");
337:     if (cJSON_IsString(session_id))
338:     {
339:         session_id_ = session_id->valuestring;
340:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Session ID: %s", session_id_.c_str());
341:     }
342: 
343:     // Get sample rate from hello message
344:     auto audio_params = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "audio_params");
345:     if (cJSON_IsObject(audio_params))
346:     {
347:         auto sample_rate = cJSON_GetObjectItem(audio_params, "sample_rate");
348:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(sample_rate))
349:         {
350:             server_sample_rate_ = sample_rate->valueint;
351:         }
352:         auto frame_duration = cJSON_GetObjectItem(audio_params, "frame_duration");
353:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(frame_duration))
354:         {
355:             server_frame_duration_ = frame_duration->valueint;
356:         }
357:     }
358: 
359:     auto udp = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "udp");
360:     if (!cJSON_IsObject(udp))
361:     {
362:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "UDP is not specified");
363:         return;
364:     }
365:     udp_server_ = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "server")->valuestring;
366:     udp_port_ = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "port")->valueint;
367:     auto key = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "key")->valuestring;
368:     auto nonce = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "nonce")->valuestring;
369: 
370:     // auto encryption = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "encryption")->valuestring;
371:     // ESP_LOGI(TAG, "UDP server: %s, port: %d, encryption: %s", udp_server_.c_str(), udp_port_, encryption);
372:     aes_nonce_ = DecodeHexString(nonce);
373:     mbedtls_aes_init(&aes_ctx_);
374:     mbedtls_aes_setkey_enc(&aes_ctx_, (const unsigned char *)DecodeHexString(key).c_str(), 128);
375:     local_sequence_ = 0;
376:     remote_sequence_ = 0;
377:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_handle_, MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT);
378: }
379: 
380: static const char hex_chars[] = "0123456789ABCDEF";
381: // 辅助函数，将单个十六进制字符转换为对应的数值
382: static inline uint8_t CharToHex(char c)
383: {
384:     if (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
385:         return c - '0';
386:     if (c >= 'A' && c <= 'F')
387:         return c - 'A' + 10;
388:     if (c >= 'a' && c <= 'f')
389:         return c - 'a' + 10;
390:     return 0; // 对于无效输入，返回0
391: }
392: 
393: std::string MqttProtocol::DecodeHexString(const std::string &hex_string)
394: {
395:     std::string decoded;
396:     decoded.reserve(hex_string.size() / 2);
397:     for (size_t i = 0; i < hex_string.size(); i += 2)
398:     {
399:         char byte = (CharToHex(hex_string[i]) << 4) | CharToHex(hex_string[i + 1]);
400:         decoded.push_back(byte);
401:     }
402:     return decoded;
403: }

405: bool MqttProtocol::IsAudioChannelOpened() const
406: {
407:     return udp_ != nullptr && !error_occurred_ && !IsTimeout();
408: }

The `MqttProtocol` destructor cleanly shuts down the MQTT communication module by stopping and deleting the reconnect timer, resetting UDP and MQTT resources, and releasing the associated event group. It ensures no pending tasks execute after shutdown by marking the instance as inactive. This function is responsible for resource cleanup and preventing memory leaks when the MQTT service is terminated.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (85): The generated code captures most of the core semantics correctly. Both codes properly handle cleanup of resources including the reconnect timer, MQTT/UDP objects, and event groups. However, there are some semantic differences: 1) The generated code lacks the critical "*alive_ = false" assignment which prevents pending scheduled tasks from executing - this is a significant semantic omission. 2) The generated code includes mutex locking for UDP reset which isn't present in ground truth, suggesting different threading assumptions. 3) The logging statement "ESP_LOGI(TAG, "MqttProtocol deinit")" is missing from generated code.

Structure Similarity (75): The overall structure shows good similarity with both following a logical cleanup sequence. Both use similar conditional checks and API calls (esp_timer_stop, esp_timer_delete, vEventGroupDelete). However, differences include: 1) Function signature formatting differs slightly. 2) The order of operations varies - ground truth sets alive flag first, while generated code handles timer first. 3) Generated code uses explicit null checks for mqtt_ and udp_ before reset() calls, while ground truth assumes these smart pointers can be unconditionally reset. 4) The mutex locking block is unique to the generated code.

Functional Completeness (80): The generated code covers most key functionalities but misses critical elements: 1) Missing the "*alive_ = false" assignment which is crucial for preventing race conditions. 2) Missing the ESP_LOGI debug log. 3) Includes additional functionality (mutex locking) not present in ground truth. 4) Properly implements timer cleanup, resource resetting, and event group deletion. The core destruction logic is sound but lacks the complete set of safety measures present in the ground truth.

results.description: The `StartMqttClient` function initializes and starts an MQTT client using configuration settings, establishing a connection to a specified broker endpoint. It sets up event handlers for connection, disconnection, and incoming messages, including parsing JSON payloads and triggering appropriate actions such as session management or audio channel control. The function also handles error reporting and scheduling of reconnect attempts upon disconnection.

302: static inline uint8_t CharToHex(char c)
303: {
304:     if (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
305:         return c - '0';
306:     if (c >= 'A' && c <= 'F')
307:         return c - 'A' + 10;
308:     if (c >= 'a' && c <= 'f')
309:         return c - 'a' + 10;
310:     return 0; // 对于无效输入，返回0
311: }

313: std::string MqttProtocol::DecodeHexString(const std::string &hex_string)
314: {
315:     std::string decoded;
316:     decoded.reserve(hex_string.size() / 2);
317:     for (size_t i = 0; i < hex_string.size(); i += 2)
318:     {
319:         char byte = (CharToHex(hex_string[i]) << 4) | CharToHex(hex_string[i + 1]);
320:         decoded.push_back(byte);
321:     }
322:     return decoded;
323: }

The `StartMqttClient` function initializes and starts an MQTT client using configuration settings, establishing a connection to a specified broker endpoint. It sets up event handlers for connection, disconnection, and incoming messages, including parsing JSON payloads and triggering appropriate actions such as session management or audio channel control. The function also handles error reporting and scheduling of reconnect attempts upon disconnection.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to implement a similar MQTT client startup process but deviates in several key areas.
- It checks for existing connections differently using IsConnected(), which is not present in the ground truth.
- Missing critical logic like parsing endpoint string to extract broker address and port.
- Differences in error handling: uses CONNECTION_ERROR instead of SERVER_NOT_FOUND/SERVER_NOT_CONNECTED.
- Reconnect timer logic differs significantly - ground truth uses fixed MQTT_RECONNECT_INTERVAL_MS while generated uses settings_.mqtt_reconnect_interval_ms.
- Session ID validation logic in goodbye message handling is missing in generated code.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches correctly.
- Both use lambda callbacks for OnConnected, OnDisconnected, and OnMessage handlers.
- General flow of setup → configuration → connection is preserved.
- However, API calls differ substantially: ground truth uses network->CreateMqtt(0), generated uses network->CreateMqtt() (no parameter).
- Ground truth configures keepalive interval explicitly; generated does not.
- Different approach to setting broker details (direct method calls vs Settings class).

Functional Completeness:
- Missing Settings loading from storage ("mqtt" section) - critical functionality absent.
- No proper endpoint parsing logic (address:port splitting).
- Lacks keepalive configuration.
- Subscribe topic logic added in generated that's not in ground truth, suggesting feature mismatch.
- Goodbye message handling lacks session ID comparison logic.
- Missing publish_topic_ assignment.
- Error reporting strings do not match expected values.
- Keepalive interval configuration is completely omitted.

results.description: The `SendText` function publishes a given text message to a predefined MQTT topic. It returns `true` if the message is successfully published, otherwise logs an error, sets a server error state, and returns `false`. This function enables reliable text-based communication over MQTT within the system's messaging framework.

The `SendText` function publishes a given text message to a predefined MQTT topic. It returns `true` if the message is successfully published, otherwise logs an error, sets a server error state, and returns `false`. This function enables reliable text-based communication over MQTT within the system's messaging framework.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (85): The generated code captures the core functionality of sending text via MQTT publish but adds additional validation logic. It correctly identifies failure conditions and sets appropriate error states. However, it uses SERVER_NOT_CONNECTED for all error cases instead of SERVER_ERROR for publish failures, which slightly deviates from the semantic intent. The additional connectivity checks make it more robust but change the original semantics somewhat.

Structure Similarity (75): The function signature matches perfectly, but the control flow differs significantly. Ground truth has a simple two-condition check followed by publish, while generated code has four conditions with additional logging. The API calls are similar (Publish, SetError, ESP_LOGE) but with different parameters and additional ESP_LOGD in generated code. The overall structure is recognizable but enhanced.

Functional Completeness (90): The generated code covers all ground truth functionality (null/client check, topic validation, publish operation, error handling, logging) and adds extra features (connection check, detailed logging, last error retrieval). It successfully implements the core requirement of sending text and handles edge cases more comprehensively than the ground truth, though with some semantic variations in error categorization.

results.description: The `SendAudio` function securely transmits an audio packet over UDP by first encrypting the payload using AES-CTR mode with a dynamically updated nonce that includes the packet size, timestamp, and sequence number. It ensures thread-safe access to the UDP channel and returns a boolean indicating whether the transmission was successful.

206: bool MqttProtocol::OpenAudioChannel()
207: {
208:     if (mqtt_ == nullptr || !mqtt_->IsConnected())
209:     {

211:         if (!StartMqttClient(true))

222:     if (!SendText(message))
223:     {

225:     }
226: 
227:     // 等待服务器响应
228:     EventBits_t bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_handle_, MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
229:     if (!(bits & MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT))
230:     {
231:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to receive server hello");
232:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_TIMEOUT);
233:         return false;
234:     }

236:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(channel_mutex_);
237:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
238:     udp_ = network->CreateUdp(2);
239:     udp_->OnMessage([this](const std::string &data)
240:                     {
241:         /*
242:          * UDP Encrypted OPUS Packet Format:
243:          * |type 1u|flags 1u|payload_len 2u|ssrc 4u|timestamp 4u|sequence 4u|
244:          * |payload payload_len|
245:          */
246:         if (data.size() < sizeof(aes_nonce_)) {
247:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid audio packet size: %u", data.size());
248:             return;
249:         }
250:         if (data[0] != 0x01) {
251:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid audio packet type: %x", data[0]);
252:             return;
253:         }
254:         uint32_t timestamp = ntohl(*(uint32_t*)&data[8]);
255:         uint32_t sequence = ntohl(*(uint32_t*)&data[12]);
256:         if (sequence < remote_sequence_) {
257:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Received audio packet with old sequence: %lu, expected: %lu", sequence, remote_sequence_);
258:             return;
259:         }
260:         if (sequence != remote_sequence_ + 1) {
261:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Received audio packet with wrong sequence: %lu, expected: %lu", sequence, remote_sequence_ + 1);
262:         }
263: 
264:         size_t decrypted_size = data.size() - aes_nonce_.size();
265:         size_t nc_off = 0;
266:         uint8_t stream_block[16] = {0};
267:         auto nonce = (uint8_t*)data.data();
268:         auto encrypted = (uint8_t*)data.data() + aes_nonce_.size();
269:         auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
270:         packet->sample_rate = server_sample_rate_;
271:         packet->frame_duration = server_frame_duration_;
272:         packet->timestamp = timestamp;
273:         packet->payload.resize(decrypted_size);
274:         int ret = mbedtls_aes_crypt_ctr(&aes_ctx_, decrypted_size, &nc_off, nonce, stream_block, encrypted, (uint8_t*)packet->payload.data());
275:         if (ret != 0) {
276:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to decrypt audio data, ret: %d", ret);
277:             return;
278:         }
279:         if (on_incoming_audio_ != nullptr) {
280:             on_incoming_audio_(std::move(packet));
281:         }
282:         remote_sequence_ = sequence;
283:         last_incoming_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now(); });
284: 
285:     udp_->Connect(udp_server_, udp_port_);
286: 
287:     if (on_audio_channel_opened_ != nullptr)
288:     {
289:         on_audio_channel_opened_();
290:     }
291:     return true;
292: }

The `SendAudio` function securely transmits an audio packet over UDP by first encrypting the payload using AES-CTR mode with a dynamically updated nonce that includes the packet size, timestamp, and sequence number. It ensures thread-safe access to the UDP channel and returns a boolean indicating whether the transmission was successful.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- Both implementations aim to send an audio packet over UDP with encryption using AES-CTR.
- The core logic of encryption and sending data is preserved.
- However, there are differences in how headers are constructed; the generated code uses a more structured header format (type, flags, length, SSRC, timestamp, sequence), while the ground truth embeds metadata directly into a nonce.
- The use of mutex locking is consistent but placed differently (early in ground truth vs after null check in generated).

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Control flow differs significantly due to different approaches in constructing the UDP packet header.
- Ground truth modifies the nonce in-place before copying, whereas generated constructs a full header explicitly.
- Use of mbedtls_aes_crypt_ctr is similar but parameter handling shows structural divergence.
- Logging messages differ slightly in content and placement.

Functional Completeness:
- Generated code includes all necessary steps: null checks, locking, encryption, and sending.
- It also adds additional functionality such as updating last_outgoing_time_, which is not in the ground truth but doesn't detract from completeness.
- One notable difference is that the payload length in the nonce is handled differently – in ground truth it's embedded in the nonce, while in generated it’s part of both the explicit header and used in nonce modification for encryption.
- Overall, functional coverage is high, though implementation details vary.

183: bool MqttProtocol::SendAudio(std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> packet)
184: {
185:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(channel_mutex_);
186:     if (udp_ == nullptr)
187:     {
188:         return false;
189:     }
190: 
191:     std::string nonce(aes_nonce_);
192:     *(uint16_t *)&nonce[2] = htons(packet->payload.size());
193:     *(uint32_t *)&nonce[8] = htonl(packet->timestamp);
194:     *(uint32_t *)&nonce[12] = htonl(++local_sequence_);
195: 
196:     std::string encrypted;
197:     encrypted.resize(aes_nonce_.size() + packet->payload.size());
198:     memcpy(encrypted.data(), nonce.data(), nonce.size());
199: 
200:     size_t nc_off = 0;
201:     uint8_t stream_block[16] = {0};
202:     if (mbedtls_aes_crypt_ctr(&aes_ctx_, packet->payload.size(), &nc_off, (uint8_t *)nonce.c_str(), stream_block,
203:                               (uint8_t *)packet->payload.data(), (uint8_t *)&encrypted[nonce.size()]) != 0)
204:     {
205:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to encrypt audio data");
206:         return false;
207:     }
208: 
209:     return udp_->Send(encrypted) > 0;
210: }

216: bool MqttProtocol::OpenAudioChannel()
217: {
218:     if (mqtt_ == nullptr || !mqtt_->IsConnected())
219:     {
220:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "MQTT is not connected, try to connect now");
221:         if (!StartMqttClient(true))
222:         {
223:             return false;
224:         }
225:     }
226: 
227:     error_occurred_ = false;
228:     session_id_ = "";
229:     xEventGroupClearBits(event_group_handle_, MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT);
230: 
231:     auto message = GetHelloMessage();
232:     if (!SendText(message))
233:     {
234:         return false;
235:     }
236: 
237:     // 等待服务器响应
238:     EventBits_t bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_handle_, MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
239:     if (!(bits & MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT))
240:     {
241:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to receive server hello");
242:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_TIMEOUT);
243:         return false;
244:     }
245: 
246:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(channel_mutex_);
247:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
248:     udp_ = network->CreateUdp(2);
249:     udp_->OnMessage([this](const std::string &data)
250:                     {
251:         /*
252:          * UDP Encrypted OPUS Packet Format:
253:          * |type 1u|flags 1u|payload_len 2u|ssrc 4u|timestamp 4u|sequence 4u|
254:          * |payload payload_len|
255:          */
256:         if (data.size() < sizeof(aes_nonce_)) {
257:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid audio packet size: %u", data.size());
258:             return;
259:         }
260:         if (data[0] != 0x01) {
261:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid audio packet type: %x", data[0]);
262:             return;
263:         }
264:         uint32_t timestamp = ntohl(*(uint32_t*)&data[8]);
265:         uint32_t sequence = ntohl(*(uint32_t*)&data[12]);
266:         if (sequence < remote_sequence_) {
267:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Received audio packet with old sequence: %lu, expected: %lu", sequence, remote_sequence_);
268:             return;
269:         }
270:         if (sequence != remote_sequence_ + 1) {
271:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Received audio packet with wrong sequence: %lu, expected: %lu", sequence, remote_sequence_ + 1);
272:         }
273: 
274:         size_t decrypted_size = data.size() - aes_nonce_.size();
275:         size_t nc_off = 0;
276:         uint8_t stream_block[16] = {0};
277:         auto nonce = (uint8_t*)data.data();
278:         auto encrypted = (uint8_t*)data.data() + aes_nonce_.size();
279:         auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
280:         packet->sample_rate = server_sample_rate_;
281:         packet->frame_duration = server_frame_duration_;
282:         packet->timestamp = timestamp;
283:         packet->payload.resize(decrypted_size);
284:         int ret = mbedtls_aes_crypt_ctr(&aes_ctx_, decrypted_size, &nc_off, nonce, stream_block, encrypted, (uint8_t*)packet->payload.data());
285:         if (ret != 0) {
286:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to decrypt audio data, ret: %d", ret);
287:             return;
288:         }
289:         if (on_incoming_audio_ != nullptr) {
290:             on_incoming_audio_(std::move(packet));
291:         }
292:         remote_sequence_ = sequence;
293:         last_incoming_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now(); });
294: 
295:     udp_->Connect(udp_server_, udp_port_);
296: 
297:     if (on_audio_channel_opened_ != nullptr)
298:     {
299:         on_audio_channel_opened_();
300:     }
301:     return true;
302: }

304: std::string MqttProtocol::GetHelloMessage()
305: {
306:     // 发送 hello 消息申请 UDP 通道
307:     cJSON *root = cJSON_CreateObject();
308:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "type", "hello");
309:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(root, "version", 3);
310:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "transport", "udp");
311:     cJSON *features = cJSON_CreateObject();
312: #if CONFIG_USE_SERVER_AEC
313:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "aec", true);
314: #endif
315:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "mcp", true);
316:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "features", features);
317:     cJSON *audio_params = cJSON_CreateObject();
318:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(audio_params, "format", "opus");
319:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "sample_rate", 16000);
320:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "channels", 1);
321:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "frame_duration", OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS);
322:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "audio_params", audio_params);
323:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root);
324:     std::string message(json_str);
325:     cJSON_free(json_str);
326:     cJSON_Delete(root);
327:     return message;
328: }

330: void MqttProtocol::ParseServerHello(const cJSON *root)
331: {
332:     auto transport = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "transport");
333:     if (transport == nullptr || strcmp(transport->valuestring, "udp") != 0)
334:     {
335:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Unsupported transport: %s", transport->valuestring);
336:         return;
337:     }
338: 
339:     auto session_id = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "session_id");
340:     if (cJSON_IsString(session_id))
341:     {
342:         session_id_ = session_id->valuestring;
343:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Session ID: %s", session_id_.c_str());
344:     }
345: 
346:     // Get sample rate from hello message
347:     auto audio_params = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "audio_params");
348:     if (cJSON_IsObject(audio_params))
349:     {
350:         auto sample_rate = cJSON_GetObjectItem(audio_params, "sample_rate");
351:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(sample_rate))
352:         {
353:             server_sample_rate_ = sample_rate->valueint;
354:         }
355:         auto frame_duration = cJSON_GetObjectItem(audio_params, "frame_duration");
356:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(frame_duration))
357:         {
358:             server_frame_duration_ = frame_duration->valueint;
359:         }
360:     }
361: 
362:     auto udp = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "udp");
363:     if (!cJSON_IsObject(udp))
364:     {
365:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "UDP is not specified");
366:         return;
367:     }
368:     udp_server_ = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "server")->valuestring;
369:     udp_port_ = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "port")->valueint;
370:     auto key = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "key")->valuestring;
371:     auto nonce = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "nonce")->valuestring;
372: 
373:     // auto encryption = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "encryption")->valuestring;
374:     // ESP_LOGI(TAG, "UDP server: %s, port: %d, encryption: %s", udp_server_.c_str(), udp_port_, encryption);
375:     aes_nonce_ = DecodeHexString(nonce);
376:     mbedtls_aes_init(&aes_ctx_);
377:     mbedtls_aes_setkey_enc(&aes_ctx_, (const unsigned char *)DecodeHexString(key).c_str(), 128);
378:     local_sequence_ = 0;
379:     remote_sequence_ = 0;
380:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_handle_, MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT);
381: }

383: static const char hex_chars[] = "0123456789ABCDEF";
384: // 辅助函数，将单个十六进制字符转换为对应的数值
385: static inline uint8_t CharToHex(char c)
386: {
387:     if (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
388:         return c - '0';
389:     if (c >= 'A' && c <= 'F')
390:         return c - 'A' + 10;
391:     if (c >= 'a' && c <= 'f')
392:         return c - 'a' + 10;
393:     return 0; // 对于无效输入，返回0
394: }

396: std::string MqttProtocol::DecodeHexString(const std::string &hex_string)
397: {
398:     std::string decoded;
399:     decoded.reserve(hex_string.size() / 2);
400:     for (size_t i = 0; i < hex_string.size(); i += 2)
401:     {
402:         char byte = (CharToHex(hex_string[i]) << 4) | CharToHex(hex_string[i + 1]);
403:         decoded.push_back(byte);
404:     }
405:     return decoded;
406: }

The `CloseAudioChannel` function terminates the active audio streaming session by resetting the UDP connection and sending a "goodbye" message to the server. It also triggers a callback notification to inform the application that the audio channel has been closed.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (85): The generated code captures the core semantics of closing the audio channel, including resetting UDP, sending a goodbye message, and triggering the callback. However, there's a key difference in the session ID handling - the ground truth always sends the goodbye message regardless of whether session_id_ is empty, while the generated code only sends it when session_id_ is not empty. This could lead to different behavior in edge cases. The generated code also unnecessarily clears the session_id_.

Structure Similarity (70): The function signature matches, but the structure differs significantly. The ground truth uses simple string concatenation for JSON creation, while the generated code uses cJSON library. The locking mechanism is slightly different in scope (the ground truth has an explicit nested scope block). Control flow is similar but organized differently with comments as section headers in the generated code.

Functional Completeness (90): The generated code covers all major functionalities: mutex locking, UDP reset, goodbye message sending, and callback execution. It even adds extra validation for session ID emptiness. However, it introduces additional complexity with cJSON library usage that wasn't in the original, and modifies the session_id_ state which wasn't part of the ground truth behavior.

results.description: The `MqttProtocol::OpenAudioChannel` function establishes a secure audio communication channel by connecting to an MQTT server, sending a hello message, and waiting for a server response. Upon successful handshake, it sets up a UDP connection to receive encrypted OPUS audio packets, which are then decrypted and passed to a callback for further processing. The function ensures proper sequencing and timing of audio data while handling potential errors such as timeouts or invalid packet sequences.

The `MqttProtocol::OpenAudioChannel` function establishes a secure audio communication channel by connecting to an MQTT server, sending a hello message, and waiting for a server response. Upon successful handshake, it sets up a UDP connection to receive encrypted OPUS audio packets, which are then decrypted and passed to a callback for further processing. The function ensures proper sequencing and timing of audio data while handling potential errors such as timeouts or invalid packet sequences.

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code attempts to implement the core logic of opening an audio channel via MQTT and setting up a UDP connection, but there are several important differences from the ground truth that affect correctness and completeness:

Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code omits the initial MQTT connection check and reconnection logic which is critical for robust operation.
- It uses a shorter timeout (5s vs 10s) for waiting on server response.
- Lacks proper error handling for network creation failure in some paths.
- Misses resetting `error_occurred_` flag and clearing event bits before sending message.
- Does not set up the UDP message handler/callback which processes incoming encrypted audio packets — this is a major functional omission.

Structure Similarity:
- General control flow follows similar steps: send hello → wait for response → setup UDP.
- Uses same APIs like `xEventGroupWaitBits`, `SendText`, and accessing board/network instances.
- Both use mutex locking when modifying shared resources (`channel_mutex_`).
- Differences include function call ordering and missing reconnect logic at start.

Functional Completeness:
- Missing critical functionality: no decryption or processing of incoming UDP audio packets using AES-CTR mode.
- No registration of `on_incoming_audio_` callback invocation.
- No tracking of timestamps/sequence numbers beyond basic initialization.
- No handling of malformed or out-of-order packets.
- Fails to invoke `on_audio_channel_opened_` after successful setup.
- Incorrectly assumes `network->CreateUdp()` takes no parameters whereas ground truth passes `2`.

Overall, while the high-level intent is captured, significant parts of the protocol implementation related to security, synchronization, and full lifecycle management are absent in the generated version.

results.description: The `GetHelloMessage` function generates a JSON-formatted MQTT hello message to request a UDP channel for audio communication. It includes device capabilities such as AEC and MCP support, along with audio parameters like format, sample rate, and frame duration. This message is used to initiate connection and negotiate features with the server.

The `GetHelloMessage` function generates a JSON-formatted MQTT hello message to request a UDP channel for audio communication. It includes device capabilities such as AEC and MCP support, along with audio parameters like format, sample rate, and frame duration. This message is used to initiate connection and negotiate features with the server.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to create a JSON hello message, which aligns with the intent of the ground truth.
- However, there are semantic differences:
  - The generated code uses "capabilities" instead of "features" as the key for the feature object.
  - It adds extra fields like "device_info" which are not part of the ground truth.
  - Sample rate is set to 48000 instead of 16000, and frame duration is hardcoded rather than using a macro.
  - The version field is moved into device_info instead of being a top-level field with value 3.

Structure Similarity:
- Both codes use cJSON to build a JSON object with a similar hierarchical structure.
- Function signature matches.
- Basic flow of creating objects, adding items, printing, and cleanup is preserved.
- Differences include different variable names, additional sections (like device_info), and placement of certain fields.

Functional Completeness:
- Missing elements from ground truth:
  - Top-level "version": 3 field.
  - "transport": "udp" field.
  - Conditional compilation for AEC (#if CONFIG_USE_SERVER_AEC).
  - Proper handling of audio parameters (channels=1, using OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS macro).
- Extra elements added that aren't in ground truth:
  - device_info section with device_id and version.
- Some core functionality overlaps but lacks precision in matching required protocol fields.

It also retrieves audio parameters such as sample rate and frame duration, initializes AES encryption, and signals that the server hello has been successfully processed. This enables secure real-time audio communication over UDP.

66: bool MqttProtocol::StartMqttClient(bool report_error)
67: {
68:     if (mqtt_ != nullptr)
69:     {
70:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Mqtt client already started");
71:         mqtt_.reset();
72:     }
73: 
74:     Settings settings("mqtt", false);
75:     auto endpoint = settings.GetString("endpoint");
76:     auto client_id = settings.GetString("client_id");
77:     auto username = settings.GetString("username");
78:     auto password = settings.GetString("password");
79:     int keepalive_interval = settings.GetInt("keepalive", 240);
80:     publish_topic_ = settings.GetString("publish_topic");
81: 
82:     if (endpoint.empty())
83:     {
84:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "MQTT endpoint is not specified");
85:         if (report_error)
86:         {
87:             SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_NOT_FOUND);
88:         }
89:         return false;
90:     }
91: 
92:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
93:     mqtt_ = network->CreateMqtt(0);
94:     mqtt_->SetKeepAlive(keepalive_interval);
95: 
96:     mqtt_->OnDisconnected([this]()
97:                           {
98:         if (on_disconnected_ != nullptr) {
99:             on_disconnected_();
100:         }
101:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "MQTT disconnected, schedule reconnect in %d seconds", MQTT_RECONNECT_INTERVAL_MS / 1000);
102:         esp_timer_start_once(reconnect_timer_, MQTT_RECONNECT_INTERVAL_MS * 1000); });
103: 
104:     mqtt_->OnConnected([this]()
105:                        {
106:         if (on_connected_ != nullptr) {
107:             on_connected_();
108:         }
109:         esp_timer_stop(reconnect_timer_); });
110: 
111:     mqtt_->OnMessage([this](const std::string &topic, const std::string &payload)
112:                      {
113:         cJSON* root = cJSON_Parse(payload.c_str());
114:         if (root == nullptr) {
115:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to parse json message %s", payload.c_str());
116:             return;
117:         }
118:         cJSON* type = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "type");
119:         if (!cJSON_IsString(type)) {
120:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Message type is invalid");
121:             cJSON_Delete(root);
122:             return;
123:         }
124: 
125:         if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "hello") == 0) {
126:             ParseServerHello(root);
127:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "goodbye") == 0) {
128:             auto session_id = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "session_id");
129:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Received goodbye message, session_id: %s", session_id ? session_id->valuestring : "null");
130:             if (session_id == nullptr || session_id_ == session_id->valuestring) {
131:                 auto alive = alive_;  // Capture alive flag
132:                 Application::GetInstance().Schedule([this, alive]() {
133:                     if (*alive) {
134:                         CloseAudioChannel();
135:                     }
136:                 });
137:             }
138:         } else if (on_incoming_json_ != nullptr) {
139:             on_incoming_json_(root);
140:         }
141:         cJSON_Delete(root);
142:         last_incoming_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now(); });
143: 
144:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Connecting to endpoint %s", endpoint.c_str());
145:     std::string broker_address;
146:     int broker_port = 8883;
147:     size_t pos = endpoint.find(':');
148:     if (pos != std::string::npos)
149:     {
150:         broker_address = endpoint.substr(0, pos);
151:         broker_port = std::stoi(endpoint.substr(pos + 1));
152:     }
153:     else
154:     {
155:         broker_address = endpoint;
156:     }
157:     if (!mqtt_->Connect(broker_address, broker_port, client_id, username, password))
158:     {
159:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to connect to endpoint, code=%d", mqtt_->GetLastError());
160:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_NOT_CONNECTED);
161:         return false;
162:     }
163: 
164:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Connected to endpoint");
165:     return true;
166: }

183: bool MqttProtocol::SendAudio(std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> packet)
184: {
185:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(channel_mutex_);
186:     if (udp_ == nullptr)
187:     {
188:         return false;
189:     }
190: 
191:     std::string nonce(aes_nonce_);
192:     *(uint16_t *)&nonce[2] = htons(packet->payload.size());
193:     *(uint32_t *)&nonce[8] = htonl(packet->timestamp);
194:     *(uint32_t *)&nonce[12] = htonl(++local_sequence_);
195: 
196:     std::string encrypted;
197:     encrypted.resize(aes_nonce_.size() + packet->payload.size());
198:     memcpy(encrypted.data(), nonce.data(), nonce.size());
199: 
200:     size_t nc_off = 0;
201:     uint8_t stream_block[16] = {0};
202:     if (mbedtls_aes_crypt_ctr(&aes_ctx_, packet->payload.size(), &nc_off, (uint8_t *)nonce.c_str(), stream_block,
203:                               (uint8_t *)packet->payload.data(), (uint8_t *)&encrypted[nonce.size()]) != 0)
204:     {
205:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to encrypt audio data");
206:         return false;
207:     }
208: 
209:     return udp_->Send(encrypted) > 0;
210: }

212: void MqttProtocol::CloseAudioChannel()
213: {
214:     {
215:         std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(channel_mutex_);
216:         udp_.reset();
217:     }
218: 
219:     std::string message = "{";
220:     message += "\"session_id\":\"" + session_id_ + "\",";
221:     message += "\"type\":\"goodbye\"";
222:     message += "}";
223:     SendText(message);
224: 
225:     if (on_audio_channel_closed_ != nullptr)
226:     {
227:         on_audio_channel_closed_();
228:     }

231: bool MqttProtocol::OpenAudioChannel()
232: {
233:     if (mqtt_ == nullptr || !mqtt_->IsConnected())
234:     {
235:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "MQTT is not connected, try to connect now");
236:         if (!StartMqttClient(true))
237:         {
238:             return false;
239:         }
240:     }
241: 
242:     error_occurred_ = false;
243:     session_id_ = "";
244:     xEventGroupClearBits(event_group_handle_, MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT);
245: 
246:     auto message = GetHelloMessage();
247:     if (!SendText(message))
248:     {
249:         return false;
250:     }
251: 
252:     // 等待服务器响应
253:     EventBits_t bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_handle_, MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
254:     if (!(bits & MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT))
255:     {
256:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to receive server hello");
257:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_TIMEOUT);
258:         return false;
259:     }
260: 
261:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(channel_mutex_);
262:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
263:     udp_ = network->CreateUdp(2);
264:     udp_->OnMessage([this](const std::string &data)
265:                     {
266:         /*
267:          * UDP Encrypted OPUS Packet Format:
268:          * |type 1u|flags 1u|payload_len 2u|ssrc 4u|timestamp 4u|sequence 4u|
269:          * |payload payload_len|
270:          */
271:         if (data.size() < sizeof(aes_nonce_)) {
272:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid audio packet size: %u", data.size());
273:             return;
274:         }
275:         if (data[0] != 0x01) {
276:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid audio packet type: %x", data[0]);
277:             return;
278:         }
279:         uint32_t timestamp = ntohl(*(uint32_t*)&data[8]);
280:         uint32_t sequence = ntohl(*(uint32_t*)&data[12]);
281:         if (sequence < remote_sequence_) {
282:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Received audio packet with old sequence: %lu, expected: %lu", sequence, remote_sequence_);
283:             return;
284:         }
285:         if (sequence != remote_sequence_ + 1) {
286:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Received audio packet with wrong sequence: %lu, expected: %lu", sequence, remote_sequence_ + 1);
287:         }
288: 
289:         size_t decrypted_size = data.size() - aes_nonce_.size();
290:         size_t nc_off = 0;
291:         uint8_t stream_block[16] = {0};
292:         auto nonce = (uint8_t*)data.data();
293:         auto encrypted = (uint8_t*)data.data() + aes_nonce_.size();
294:         auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
295:         packet->sample_rate = server_sample_rate_;
296:         packet->frame_duration = server_frame_duration_;
297:         packet->timestamp = timestamp;
298:         packet->payload.resize(decrypted_size);
299:         int ret = mbedtls_aes_crypt_ctr(&aes_ctx_, decrypted_size, &nc_off, nonce, stream_block, encrypted, (uint8_t*)packet->payload.data());
300:         if (ret != 0) {
301:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to decrypt audio data, ret: %d", ret);
302:             return;
303:         }
304:         if (on_incoming_audio_ != nullptr) {
305:             on_incoming_audio_(std::move(packet));
306:         }
307:         remote_sequence_ = sequence;
308:         last_incoming_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now(); });
309: 
310:     udp_->Connect(udp_server_, udp_port_);
311: 
312:     if (on_audio_channel_opened_ != nullptr)
313:     {
314:         on_audio_channel_opened_();
315:     }
316:     return true;
317: }

319: std::string MqttProtocol::GetHelloMessage()
320: {
321:     // 发送 hello 消息申请 UDP 通道
322:     cJSON *root = cJSON_CreateObject();
323:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "type", "hello");
324:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(root, "version", 3);
325:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "transport", "udp");
326:     cJSON *features = cJSON_CreateObject();
327: #if CONFIG_USE_SERVER_AEC
328:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "aec", true);
329: #endif
330:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "mcp", true);
331:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "features", features);
332:     cJSON *audio_params = cJSON_CreateObject();
333:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(audio_params, "format", "opus");
334:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "sample_rate", 16000);
335:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "channels", 1);
336:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "frame_duration", OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS);
337:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "audio_params", audio_params);
338:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root);
339:     std::string message(json_str);
340:     cJSON_free(json_str);
341:     cJSON_Delete(root);
342:     return message;
343: }

349: static const char hex_chars[] = "0123456789ABCDEF";
350: // 辅助函数，将单个十六进制字符转换为对应的数值
351: static inline uint8_t CharToHex(char c)
352: {
353:     if (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
354:         return c - '0';
355:     if (c >= 'A' && c <= 'F')
356:         return c - 'A' + 10;
357:     if (c >= 'a' && c <= 'f')
358:         return c - 'a' + 10;
359:     return 0; // 对于无效输入，返回0
360: }
361: 
362: std::string MqttProtocol::DecodeHexString(const std::string &hex_string)
363: {
364:     std::string decoded;
365:     decoded.reserve(hex_string.size() / 2);
366:     for (size_t i = 0; i < hex_string.size(); i += 2)
367:     {
368:         char byte = (CharToHex(hex_string[i]) << 4) | CharToHex(hex_string[i + 1]);
369:         decoded.push_back(byte);
370:     }
371:     return decoded;
372: }

374: bool MqttProtocol::IsAudioChannelOpened() const
375: {
376:     return udp_ != nullptr && !error_occurred_ && !IsTimeout();
377: }

</file>
The `ParseServerHello` function processes the server's hello message to extract and configure UDP transport settings, including the server address, port, encryption key, and nonce. It also retrieves audio parameters such as sample rate and frame duration, initializes AES encryption, and signals that the server hello has been successfully processed. This enables secure real-time audio communication over UDP.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to parse a server hello message and extract necessary fields like UDP server info, encryption details, and audio parameters.
- However, there are significant semantic differences:
  - It looks for "server_addr" and "server_port" instead of parsing a nested "udp" object as in the ground truth.
  - It assumes direct key/nonce fields rather than extracting them from within an "udp" sub-object.
  - No validation of transport type ("transport" field is completely ignored).
  - Error handling (e.g., checking for null or unsupported values) is missing.
  - Remote/local sequence numbers are not initialized in the generated code.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches.
- Uses cJSON APIs similarly but applies them differently due to incorrect field names.
- Control flow diverges significantly; e.g., no early returns on error conditions, different ordering of operations.
- Ground truth uses more defensive programming patterns which are absent in the generated version.

Functional Completeness:
- Partially covers core functionality such as setting up AES context, decoding hex strings, and extracting audio parameters.
- Misses critical elements:
  - Transport protocol validation.
  - Proper parsing of nested JSON structures (especially the 'udp' object).
  - Logging mechanisms.
  - Initialization of sequence counters.
  - Event group signaling appears but lacks preceding validations.
- Overall, while some components align conceptually, many essential behaviors and data extractions differ structurally and semantically.

231: bool MqttProtocol::OpenAudioChannel()
232: {
233:     if (mqtt_ == nullptr || !mqtt_->IsConnected())
234:     {
235:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "MQTT is not connected, try to connect now");
236:         if (!StartMqttClient(true))

239:         }

242:     error_occurred_ = false;
243:     session_id_ = "";

245: 
246:     auto message = GetHelloMessage();
247:     if (!SendText(message))
248:     {
249:         return false;
250:     }
251: 
252:     // 等待服务器响应
253:     EventBits_t bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_handle_, MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
254:     if (!(bits & MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT))
255:     {
256:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to receive server hello");
257:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_TIMEOUT);
258:         return false;
259:     }
260: 
261:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(channel_mutex_);
262:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
263:     udp_ = network->CreateUdp(2);
264:     udp_->OnMessage([this](const std::string &data)
265:                     {
266:         /*
267:          * UDP Encrypted OPUS Packet Format:
268:          * |type 1u|flags 1u|payload_len 2u|ssrc 4u|timestamp 4u|sequence 4u|
269:          * |payload payload_len|
270:          */
271:         if (data.size() < sizeof(aes_nonce_)) {
272:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid audio packet size: %u", data.size());
273:             return;
274:         }
275:         if (data[0] != 0x01) {
276:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid audio packet type: %x", data[0]);
277:             return;
278:         }
279:         uint32_t timestamp = ntohl(*(uint32_t*)&data[8]);
280:         uint32_t sequence = ntohl(*(uint32_t*)&data[12]);
281:         if (sequence < remote_sequence_) {
282:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Received audio packet with old sequence: %lu, expected: %lu", sequence, remote_sequence_);
283:             return;
284:         }
285:         if (sequence != remote_sequence_ + 1) {
286:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Received audio packet with wrong sequence: %lu, expected: %lu", sequence, remote_sequence_ + 1);
287:         }
288: 
289:         size_t decrypted_size = data.size() - aes_nonce_.size();
290:         size_t nc_off = 0;
291:         uint8_t stream_block[16] = {0};
292:         auto nonce = (uint8_t*)data.data();
293:         auto encrypted = (uint8_t*)data.data() + aes_nonce_.size();
294:         auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
295:         packet->sample_rate = server_sample_rate_;
296:         packet->frame_duration = server_frame_duration_;
297:         packet->timestamp = timestamp;
298:         packet->payload.resize(decrypted_size);
299:         int ret = mbedtls_aes_crypt_ctr(&aes_ctx_, decrypted_size, &nc_off, nonce, stream_block, encrypted, (uint8_t*)packet->payload.data());

301:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to decrypt audio data, ret: %d", ret);
302:             return;
303:         }
304:         if (on_incoming_audio_ != nullptr) {
305:             on_incoming_audio_(std::move(packet));
306:         }
307:         remote_sequence_ = sequence;
308:         last_incoming_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now(); });

310:     udp_->Connect(udp_server_, udp_port_);
311: 
312:     if (on_audio_channel_opened_ != nullptr)
313:     {
314:         on_audio_channel_opened_();
315:     }
316:     return true;

345: void MqttProtocol::ParseServerHello(const cJSON *root)
346: {
347:     auto transport = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "transport");
348:     if (transport == nullptr || strcmp(transport->valuestring, "udp") != 0)
349:     {
350:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Unsupported transport: %s", transport->valuestring);
351:         return;
352:     }
353: 
354:     auto session_id = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "session_id");
355:     if (cJSON_IsString(session_id))
356:     {
357:         session_id_ = session_id->valuestring;
358:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Session ID: %s", session_id_.c_str());
359:     }
360: 
361:     // Get sample rate from hello message
362:     auto audio_params = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "audio_params");
363:     if (cJSON_IsObject(audio_params))
364:     {
365:         auto sample_rate = cJSON_GetObjectItem(audio_params, "sample_rate");
366:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(sample_rate))
367:         {
368:             server_sample_rate_ = sample_rate->valueint;
369:         }
370:         auto frame_duration = cJSON_GetObjectItem(audio_params, "frame_duration");
371:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(frame_duration))
372:         {
373:             server_frame_duration_ = frame_duration->valueint;
374:         }
375:     }
376: 
377:     auto udp = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "udp");
378:     if (!cJSON_IsObject(udp))
379:     {
380:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "UDP is not specified");
381:         return;
382:     }
383:     udp_server_ = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "server")->valuestring;
384:     udp_port_ = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "port")->valueint;
385:     auto key = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "key")->valuestring;
386:     auto nonce = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "nonce")->valuestring;
387: 
388:     // auto encryption = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "encryption")->valuestring;
389:     // ESP_LOGI(TAG, "UDP server: %s, port: %d, encryption: %s", udp_server_.c_str(), udp_port_, encryption);
390:     aes_nonce_ = DecodeHexString(nonce);
391:     mbedtls_aes_init(&aes_ctx_);
392:     mbedtls_aes_setkey_enc(&aes_ctx_, (const unsigned char *)DecodeHexString(key).c_str(), 128);
393:     local_sequence_ = 0;
394:     remote_sequence_ = 0;
395:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_handle_, MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT);
396: }

404: std::string MqttProtocol::DecodeHexString(const std::string &hex_string)
405: {
406:     std::string decoded;
407:     decoded.reserve(hex_string.size() / 2);
408:     for (size_t i = 0; i < hex_string.size(); i += 2)
409:     {
410:         char byte = (CharToHex(hex_string[i]) << 4) | CharToHex(hex_string[i + 1]);
411:         decoded.push_back(byte);
412:     }
413:     return decoded;
414: }

The `MqttProtocol::DecodeHexString` function converts a hexadecimal string into its corresponding binary data representation. It processes the input string two characters at a time, interpreting each pair as a hexadecimal value and converting it into a byte, which is then appended to the output string. This function is typically used to decode encoded payloads or identifiers in MQTT communication.

functions: std::string::size (used as length() instead of size())
variables: decoded (not used; result is used instead)
byte (not explicitly declared, though byte_value serves the purpose)

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- Both implementations correctly decode a hexadecimal string into its binary representation by processing two characters at a time.
- The core logic of combining high and low nibbles using bitwise operations is preserved.
- The generated code includes additional safety checks for edge cases (e.g., odd-length strings), which enhances robustness without altering the primary function semantics.
- Minor difference in variable types (`char` vs `uint8_t`) does not affect correctness but improves clarity in the generated version.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly, including class scoping and parameter type.
- Control flow is similar: both use a loop that increments by 2 and perform nibble conversion followed by combination.
- Key API usage like `CharToHex`, bit shifting, and `push_back` are consistent.
- Differences include formatting (e.g., brace style), explicit length checking in the generated code, and early exit handling for invalid input which adds structural divergence.

Functional Completeness:
- The generated code covers all essential functionality found in the ground truth: reserving/appending to result string, converting hex chars to bytes, and returning the decoded output.
- It also introduces improved error resilience by explicitly checking bounds before accessing elements, an enhancement over the ground truth which assumes valid even-length input.
- Missing optimization from ground truth: `reserve()` call to preallocate memory in the generated version may lead to minor performance differences under some conditions.

The `Protocol::IsTimeout()` function checks whether the time elapsed since the last incoming message exceeds a predefined threshold of 120 seconds. If the timeout condition is met, it logs an error message indicating the channel has timed out and returns `true`; otherwise, it returns `false`. This function is used to detect and handle inactive or unresponsive communication channels.

224: std::string WebsocketProtocol::GetHelloMessage()
225: {
226:     // keys: message type, version, audio_params (format, sample_rate, channels)
227:     cJSON *root = cJSON_CreateObject();
228:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "type", "hello");
229:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(root, "version", version_);
230:     cJSON *features = cJSON_CreateObject();
231: #if CONFIG_USE_SERVER_AEC
232:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "aec", true);
233: #endif
234:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "mcp", true);
235:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "features", features);
236:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "transport", "websocket");
237:     cJSON *audio_params = cJSON_CreateObject();
238:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(audio_params, "format", "opus");
239:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "sample_rate", 16000);
240:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "channels", 1);
241:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "frame_duration", OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS);
242:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "audio_params", audio_params);
243:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root);
244:     std::string message(json_str);
245:     cJSON_free(json_str);
246:     cJSON_Delete(root);
247:     return message;
248: }

The `SendAudio` function transmits audio data over a WebSocket connection using version-specific binary protocols (v2 or v3) or raw payload format based on the negotiated protocol version. It ensures reliable delivery by checking the WebSocket connection status before sending and formats the audio packet with appropriate headers and metadata. The function supports real-time audio streaming with timestamping and payload size encoding depending on the protocol version.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The general logic of checking connection status and handling different protocol versions is preserved.
- However, there are critical semantic differences:
  * In version 2, the `type` field is set to 1 in generated code vs 0 in ground truth.
  * Version 2 includes a `reserved` field in ground truth but not initialized in generated code.
  * Version 3 omits `reserved` field initialization and uses direct assignment instead of network byte order for `type`.
  * Error handling differs significantly - ground truth silently fails while generated code logs errors and sets error state.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Control flow follows similar pattern: null/connected check → version branching → send operation.
- Uses similar memory management approach with resize-and-copy pattern.
- Both use htons/htonl for network byte ordering (though applied differently).
- Major structural difference is use of std::vector<uint8_t> vs std::string for buffer management.

Functional Completeness:
- Missing key functionality: proper handling of version 2's 'reserved' field.
- Missing key functionality: consistent type field values across versions.
- Additional functionality in generated code (logging, SetError) not present in ground truth.
- Core sending mechanism is implemented but with incorrect protocol field values.
- Fails to handle edge cases identically (e.g., what happens when version is neither 2 nor 3).

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to replicate core functionality such as creating a WebSocket, setting headers, handling binary data based on protocol version, and sending a hello message.
- However, there are significant deviations in implementation details that affect correctness:
  - Uses `std::make_unique<WebSocket>` instead of `network->CreateWebSocket`, which changes resource management semantics.
  - Authentication logic differs—does not check for existing space in token before prepending "Bearer".
  - Header names differ significantly (e.g., "X-Device-ID" vs "Device-Id").
  - Missing critical parts like parsing JSON messages ("hello" type), calling specific callbacks (`on_incoming_json_`, `on_audio_channel_opened_`), and using event groups to wait for server responses.
  - Incorrect casting and processing of binary protocols without proper validation or endianness handling in some cases.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches but internal structure diverges considerably.
- Control flow is simplified and omits several conditional checks and error-handling paths found in the ground truth.
- Different APIs used throughout (e.g., `SetHeaders(map)` vs individual `SetHeader()` calls).
- Callback registration differs in approach and capability (`SetDataHandler` vs `OnData` with separate text/binary handling).
- Lacks use of singleton patterns (`Board::GetInstance()`) and system utilities (`SystemInfo::GetMacAddress()`).

Functional Completeness:
- Covers basic WebSocket creation, header setup, and binary/audio packet processing.
- Partially implements protocol versioning logic for v2 and v3 but misses edge cases and robustness features.
- Omits essential components such as:
  - Reading configuration from `Settings`.
  - Proper error reporting via `SetError()` and logging error codes.
  - Handling disconnection events with callback invocation.
  - Waiting for server hello acknowledgment using FreeRTOS event groups.
  - Invoking channel open/close callbacks.
- Introduces assumptions not present in the original (like member variables `server_url_`, `auth_token_`) which may not reflect actual class design.

79:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to send text: %s", text.c_str());
80:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_ERROR);
81:         return false;
82:     }
83: 
84:     return true;
85: }

87: bool WebsocketProtocol::IsAudioChannelOpened() const
88: {
89:     return websocket_ != nullptr && websocket_->IsConnected() && !error_occurred_ && !IsTimeout();
90: }

92: void WebsocketProtocol::CloseAudioChannel()
93: {

97: bool WebsocketProtocol::OpenAudioChannel()
98: {
99:     Settings settings("websocket", false);
100:     std::string url = settings.GetString("url");
101:     std::string token = settings.GetString("token");
102:     int version = settings.GetInt("version");
103:     if (version != 0)
104:     {
105:         version_ = version;
106:     }
107: 
108:     error_occurred_ = false;
109: 
110:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
111:     websocket_ = network->CreateWebSocket(1);
112:     if (websocket_ == nullptr)
113:     {
114:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to create websocket");
115:         return false;
116:     }
117: 
118:     if (!token.empty())
119:     {
120:         // If token not has a space, add "Bearer " prefix
121:         if (token.find(" ") == std::string::npos)
122:         {
123:             token = "Bearer " + token;
124:         }
125:         websocket_->SetHeader("Authorization", token.c_str());
126:     }
127:     websocket_->SetHeader("Protocol-Version", std::to_string(version_).c_str());
128:     websocket_->SetHeader("Device-Id", SystemInfo::GetMacAddress().c_str());
129:     websocket_->SetHeader("Client-Id", Board::GetInstance().GetUuid().c_str());
130: 
131:     websocket_->OnData([this](const char *data, size_t len, bool binary)
132:                        {
133:         if (binary) {
134:             if (on_incoming_audio_ != nullptr) {
135:                 if (version_ == 2) {
136:                     BinaryProtocol2* bp2 = (BinaryProtocol2*)data;
137:                     bp2->version = ntohs(bp2->version);
138:                     bp2->type = ntohs(bp2->type);
139:                     bp2->timestamp = ntohl(bp2->timestamp);
140:                     bp2->payload_size = ntohl(bp2->payload_size);
141:                     auto payload = (uint8_t*)bp2->payload;
142:                     on_incoming_audio_(std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>(AudioStreamPacket{
143:                         .sample_rate = server_sample_rate_,
144:                         .frame_duration = server_frame_duration_,
145:                         .timestamp = bp2->timestamp,
146:                         .payload = std::vector<uint8_t>(payload, payload + bp2->payload_size)
147:                     }));
148:                 } else if (version_ == 3) {
149:                     BinaryProtocol3* bp3 = (BinaryProtocol3*)data;
150:                     bp3->type = bp3->type;
151:                     bp3->payload_size = ntohs(bp3->payload_size);
152:                     auto payload = (uint8_t*)bp3->payload;
153:                     on_incoming_audio_(std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>(AudioStreamPacket{
154:                         .sample_rate = server_sample_rate_,
155:                         .frame_duration = server_frame_duration_,
156:                         .timestamp = 0,
157:                         .payload = std::vector<uint8_t>(payload, payload + bp3->payload_size)
158:                     }));
159:                 } else {
160:                     on_incoming_audio_(std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>(AudioStreamPacket{
161:                         .sample_rate = server_sample_rate_,
162:                         .frame_duration = server_frame_duration_,
163:                         .timestamp = 0,
164:                         .payload = std::vector<uint8_t>((uint8_t*)data, (uint8_t*)data + len)
165:                     }));
166:                 }
167:             }
168:         } else {
169:             // Parse JSON data
170:             auto root = cJSON_Parse(data);
171:             auto type = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "type");
172:             if (cJSON_IsString(type)) {
173:                 if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "hello") == 0) {
174:                     ParseServerHello(root);
175:                 } else {
176:                     if (on_incoming_json_ != nullptr) {
177:                         on_incoming_json_(root);
178:                     }
179:                 }
180:             } else {
181:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Missing message type, data: %s", data);
182:             }
183:             cJSON_Delete(root);
184:         }
185:         last_incoming_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now(); });
186: 
187:     websocket_->OnDisconnected([this]()
188:                                {
189:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Websocket disconnected");
190:         if (on_audio_channel_closed_ != nullptr) {
191:             on_audio_channel_closed_();
192:         } });
193: 
194:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Connecting to websocket server: %s with version: %d", url.c_str(), version_);
195:     if (!websocket_->Connect(url.c_str()))
196:     {
197:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to connect to websocket server, code=%d", websocket_->GetLastError());
198:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_NOT_CONNECTED);
199:         return false;
200:     }
201: 
202:     // Send hello message to describe the client
203:     auto message = GetHelloMessage();
204:     if (!SendText(message))
205:     {
206:         return false;
207:     }
208: 
209:     // Wait for server hello
210:     EventBits_t bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_handle_, WEBSOCKET_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
211:     if (!(bits & WEBSOCKET_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT))
212:     {
213:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to receive server hello");
214:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_TIMEOUT);
215:         return false;
216:     }
217: 
218:     if (on_audio_channel_opened_ != nullptr)
219:     {
220:         on_audio_channel_opened_();
221:     }
222: 
223:     return true;
224: }

226: std::string WebsocketProtocol::GetHelloMessage()
227: {
228:     // keys: message type, version, audio_params (format, sample_rate, channels)
229:     cJSON *root = cJSON_CreateObject();
230:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "type", "hello");
231:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(root, "version", version_);
232:     cJSON *features = cJSON_CreateObject();
233: #if CONFIG_USE_SERVER_AEC
234:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "aec", true);
235: #endif
236:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "mcp", true);
237:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "features", features);
238:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "transport", "websocket");
239:     cJSON *audio_params = cJSON_CreateObject();
240:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(audio_params, "format", "opus");
241:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "sample_rate", 16000);
242:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "channels", 1);
243:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "frame_duration", OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS);
244:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "audio_params", audio_params);
245:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root);
246:     std::string message(json_str);
247:     cJSON_free(json_str);
248:     cJSON_Delete(root);
249:     return message;
250: }

252: void WebsocketProtocol::ParseServerHello(const cJSON *root)

254: }

</file>
The `ParseServerHello` function processes the server's initial handshake response in a WebSocket connection, extracting and storing session identification and audio configuration parameters such as sample rate and frame duration. It validates the transport type and sets an event flag upon successful parsing to signal readiness for further communication. This enables the device to configure its audio streaming settings based on server-provided specifications.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code captures most of the core logic from the ground truth but differs in some important behaviors.
- It correctly parses session_id, audio_params (sample_rate and frame_duration), and sets the event group bit.
- However, it handles the "transport" validation differently — instead of returning early on unsupported transport, it only logs a warning. This changes the execution semantics significantly.
- Uses `valuedouble` instead of `valueint`, which may be more accurate for floating point numbers, but inconsistent with the ground truth's integer usage.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Control flow is reordered compared to the ground truth: session parsing comes first, followed by audio params, then transport check, whereas the original checks transport first and exits early if invalid.
- Uses similar cJSON API calls (`cGetObjectItem`, `cJSON_IsString`, etc.), but order and conditional structure differ.
- Logging behavior is slightly different; uses ESP_LOGW instead of ESP_LOGE and omits logging the session ID.

Functional Completeness:
- Covers main functionality including session ID assignment, audio parameter extraction, and setting event bits.
- Misses explicit error handling for unsupported transports (no early return).
- Lacks detailed logging such as printing the session ID.
- Overall feature coverage is good but misses critical error handling path that affects program behavior.
